questions.explanation: This infant would receive one point for skin color (appearance), one point for pulse rate, zero for grimace, one point for activity, and one point for respirations.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: When should you expect an infant to start to breathe spontaneously?
options: one to five seconds after birth
questions.explanation: An infant normally starts breathing independently within about 20–30 seconds after birth; if it does not, provide tactile -stimulations.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of these situations is a true emergency that cannot be managed in the field?
questions.explanation: A limb presentation, in which an arm or leg is the presenting part, is a true emergency -situation that cannot be managed in the field. Place the mother with her pelvis elevated, exert gentle pressure on the baby’s body, and transport the mother in this position. Breech, meconium, and multiple births can all be handled in the field. Call for ALS assistance for each of these emergencies and expect fetal distress in the newborn(s).
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Your patient is a 19-year-old female with severe vaginal bleeding. As part of the SAMPLE history, it is particularly important to ask the patient if she
questions.explanation: Determine if the patient may be pregnant, and if so, treat her like a pregnant woman—administer oxygen and transport her on her left side.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: All of the following are examples of indirect medical direction EXCEPT
options: continuing education classes taught by physicians.
contact between physicians and EMTs in the field.
design of EMS systems by physicians.
questions.explanation: Indirect medical direction includes all involvement by physicians with EMS systems except direct supervision of EMTs in the field.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: You should allow family members to remain with a patient EXCEPT when
options: the patient is in the process of dying.
they want to reassure the patient.
they interfere with patient care. –emt-basic practice exam 398
questions.explanation: Always allow family members to stay with a patient except if their presence interferes with necessary care.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: If a newborn infant’s respiratory effort is inadequate, your first intervention should be to
options: provide artificial ventilations at a rate of 120/ min.
provide positive pressure ventilations at a rate of 60/min.
administer high-flow oxygen via the blow-by method.
transport immediately to a hospital with a newborn intensive care unit.
questions.explanation: If, on initial assessment, the newborn’s respirations are slow, shallow, or absent, you should provide positive pressure ventilations with a bag-valve mask at the rate of 60/min. After about 30 seconds of this intervention, you should reassess the patient to determine if you need to continue with the bag-valvemask ventilation.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: In which situation would you be treating a patient under an assumption of implied -consent?
options: 4-year-old male, with broken leg, parent gives permission for treatment
19-year-old female, with seizure, who refuses treatment or transport
47-year-old male, with chest pain, requests transport to the hospital
80-year-old female, unconscious, no friends or family members present
questions.explanation: Implied consent is applicable in cases where the patient is unable to consent; you provide treatment under the assumption that any rational person would want to receive treatment under the circumstances.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: A standard of care is the
options: highest level of care an EMT can legally -provide.
list of skills EMTs are required to perform in your state.
national description of all procedures the EMTs may perform.
minimum level of care that is normally provided in your locality.
questions.explanation: A standard of care for any locality is the minimum acceptable level of care normally provided there. All care providers, including EMTs, are legally held to this standard.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Which situation might constitute legal abandonment of a patient?
options: You leave your patient because the fire in an adjacent building reaches the room you are in.
You begin CPR on a patient and ask a bystander to continue while you assess another patient.
Your patient states that he or she does not want treatment and signs a statement to that effect.
You transport a patient to the hospital and leave him or her in the care of a nurse who signed your patient-care report.
questions.explanation: Abandonment may occur if you stop caring for a patient without his or her consent, and without transferring care to personnel of the same or higher level. For patient a, personal safety takes priority over patient care. If a conscious capable adult patient has signed a refusal (and he or she heard about risks/benefits first), you have not committed abandonment, ruling out case c. To avoid abandonment when dealing with hospitals, hospital personnel must assume care of patient before you can leave, and this occurred in case d.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Your patient is an adult male who was found in a state of collapse. He has no identification, and no one else is nearby. When you care for this patient, you are acting under the legal doctrine of
questions.explanation: Implied consent means that you assume that any rational person would consent to care under the circumstances.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Your patient is a 25-year-old male who has had two seizures within the last hour. He shows no signs of drug or alcohol use. He now states that he feels fine and refuses both transport and further treatment. You should
options: carefully document every attempt you make to provide care.
obtain an order for tranquilizers from the medical direction physician.
call for police backup to help you restrain the patient.
leave the patient immediately to avoid being sued for battery. –emt-basic practice exam 399
questions.explanation: Refusal of treatment requires that the patient be fully aware of the consequences of refusing care. Document all attempts made to change the patient’s mind by you and other caregivers.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: In which of the following situations might you be found to have committed assault against your patient?
options: You leave your patient at the scene of an accident before additional help arrives.
You tell the patient, “If you don’t let me splint your leg, I’ll have to tie you up.”
You tell the patient, “You’re over 18 and you have the right to refuse treatment.”
You transfer care of your patient to a first responder who has just arrived on the scene.
questions.explanation: Threatening the patient may be held to constitute assault; options a and d both constitute abandonment of a patient.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The power-lift position refers to a position in which you
options: lift a patient as high as you possibly can.
squat down, lock your back, and use your knees to lift.
bend at the waist and jerk the patient up quickly.
lift a one-rescuer stretcher by yourself.
questions.explanation: In the power lift, you squat down, lock your back to keep it straight, and use your knees to lift the required weight.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Whenever it is necessary to carry out an emergency move of a patient, it is important to
options: pull the patient along the line of the body’s long axis.
immobilize the patient to a long spine board before the move.
complete the detailed assessment before the move.
have at least two EMTs lift the patient off the ground.
questions.explanation: An emergency move is carried out when the patient is in immediate danger. Because there is no time to protect the spine before an emergency move, you should try to protect the spine by dragging the patient in a straight line, either by pulling on his or her clothing or by placing the patient on a blanket and dragging the blanket.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The first step in a rapid extrication of a patient from a car is to
options: place the end of the long backboard on the seat.
rotate the patient until his or her back is in the open doorway.
move all of the extremities into a neutral position.
questions.explanation: Before beginning the actual extrication, the first step is to check and open the airway if necessary. You need to attempt to apply a cervical collar before performing the move. If it is a true emergency (scene is very unstable and dangerous) and you cannot do this, make every effort to keep the head and neck in a neutral alignment while you move the patient. If you can slide the patient onto a longboard, it will make moving him or her easier because you can keep him or her in alignment more easily.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which patient should be placed in the recovery position?
options: pregnant woman who has been in a car accident
conscious cardiac patient complaining of shortness of breath
unresponsive patient who has a suspected spine injury
child in respiratory distress with a rate of 6/min
questions.explanation: Use the recovery position for a patient who is breathing spontaneously but who is unresponsive or likely to vomit and aspirate material into the airway. You should not use it for a spinal injured patient until he or she is properly im-mobilized. Patient a should be placed in the left lateral recumbent position once she is immobilized. Patient b should be assisted into the position of comfort with the recovery position used if the mental status decreases (and he or she is still breathing adequately). Patient d needs bag-valve-mask ventilation.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The increased pressure on arterial walls produced when the left ventricle contracts is the
questions.explanation: The systolic pressure is the increased pressure in the artery when the left ventricle is contracted.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You are called to the scene of a car crash. The rescue crew tells you that the patient, who is still trapped inside the car, is not breathing. An emergency move is not possible at this time, as the patient is pinned. After determining that the scene is safe to enter, you should
options: assist the rescue crew in extricating the patient.
open the airway while extrication is going on.
call for further backup to the scene.
perform a complete physical exam -immediately. –emt-basic practice exam 3100
questions.explanation: Always perform critical care, such as opening the patient’s airway, while extrication is occurring.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of an effective way to communicate with an elderly patient?
options: “Sir, I’m going to try to make you comfortable until we get to the hospital.”
“You just sit down here on this nice, comfortable chair and don’t worry.”
“I will try to talk very slowly so you can understand me.”
“Sir, I’ll make sure I explain everything I’m doing to your son.”
questions.explanation: Speak to elderly patients respectfully, directly, and clearly, and do not assume that they can’t understand the situation.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When healthcare professionals speak of trending, they are referring to
options: new styles and methods of care delivery.
the tendency of depressed patients to refuse treatment.
a general change in the nation’s health status.
a comparison of a patient’s present and past condition.
questions.explanation: Trending refers to a comparison of a patient’s past and present condition. Trending is always more significant than a single set of vital signs.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The bladder of a blood pressure cuff should be centered over the
options: femoral artery. Questions 116–120 refer to the following -scenario: You respond to a dispatch of an “unknown medical event.” As you arrive on the scene, an elderly gentleman greets you at the door and motions you to step inside. You are directed to the rear of the house, where an -elderly woman presents to you sitting on the bed. She appears to be in severe respiratory -distress.
questions.explanation: The blood pressure cuff, when inflated, squeezes the brachial artery until it squeezes shut. As air is released from the bladder, at some point, the pressure in the artery exceeds the pressure in the cuff, causing blood to squirt past the cuff. In order for this to be accurate, the bladder should be as closely centered over the brachial artery as possible.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: After assessing the scene for hazards, what should you do next?
options: Establish the patient’s baseline vital signs.
questions.explanation: An initial assessment will determine the initial, most serious life threats first.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: When should you place the patient on oxygen?
options: after medical command gives you permission
questions.explanation: If the patient is having respiratory distress, provide oxygen immediately after assessing her respiratory effort.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following signs would you assess for first?
questions.explanation: Since her chief complaint appears to be respiratory distress, listening to lung sounds early would provide some information about her respiratory system.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following questions would you NOT ask during your OPQRST of the present illness?
options: When did you begin having trouble breathing?
Does anything else cause you pain or -discomfort?
Does anything make the breathing easier or worse?
Do you have any respiratory problems?
questions.explanation: This is a good question to ask during the SAMPLE history taking.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: After a few minutes of supplemental oxygen therapy, the patient states that she feels much better. What would you do now?
options: Discontinue the oxygen since she feels -better.
Perform a reassessment of her vital signs.
Have her sign a refusal-of-care form.
questions.explanation: An ongoing assessment is important to perform so that you can spot trends in the patient’s condition.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: After the head emerges when delivering a baby, you should
options: guide the head downward as the upper shoulder appears.
feel at the neck to see if the umbilical cord is wrapped around it.
questions.explanation: When delivering an infant, feel around the baby’s neck after the head emerges to determine if the cord is wrapped around it. This situation, called a nuchal cord, is extremely dangerous for the baby and can cause strangulation or brain damage. The cord must be released from around the neck immediately.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: In addition to cause, duty to act, breach of duty, and damages, what other factor must be present to be considered negligent in your duties?
questions.explanation: In order to be found negligent in your duties, prudence must exist, along with cause, breach of duty, duty to act, and patient damages and/or harm.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You respond to a patient who has amputated two digits. What should you do with the amputated digits?
options: Place the digits in a biohazard bag and keep them cool with cold packs.
Place the digits in sterile saline.
Place the digits in a biohazard bag and dispose of them in the proper receptacle.
questions.explanation: Amputated digits should be placed in a biohazard bag and cold packs should be placed outside of the bag to keep them cold. Never submerge an amputated part or place it directly on ice, as damage to the tissue can occur.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: A burn that is characterized by pain, blisters, and mottled skin is a
questions.explanation: A burn that is characterized by pain, blisters, and mottled skin is a partial thickness burn.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: The upper chambers of the heart are the
questions.explanation: The upper right and left chambers of the heart are the atria.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The left side of the heart receives blood from the
questions.explanation: The left side of the heart receives oxygenated blood from the lungs through the pulmonary veins.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The body’s three-stage response to stress is referred to as the
questions.explanation: The body’s three-stage response to stress is referred to as general adaptation syndrome.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The most effective way to control disease transmission is by using
questions.explanation: The most effective way to control disease transmission is by proper hand-washing.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: If you suspect that your patient has tuberculosis, you should
options: wear a standard surgical mask when treating the patient.
place a HEPA respirator on the patient.
wear a HEPA respirator when treating the patient.
transport the patient rapidly, with the ambulance’s exhaust fan on high.
questions.explanation: If you suspect that your patient has tuberculosis, you should wear a HEPA respirator when treating the patient.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The exchange of oxygen and carbon dioxide occurs within the
questions.explanation: The exchange of gasses occurs within the alveoli in the lungs.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What is a normal systolic blood pressure for a 2-year-old?
questions.explanation: The normal systolic blood pressure for a 2-year-old child ranges from 80 to 100 mm Hg.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Cardiopulmonary arrest in children is most commonly caused by
questions.explanation: Cardiopulmonary arrest in children is most commonly caused by respiratory failure.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The EMT’s primary concern is
options: to provide fast, efficient care to injured or ill patients.
their safety and the safety of other team members.
questions.explanation: The primary concern of EMTs is their safety and the safety of their crew. Under no circumstances should patient care come before scene safety.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: The manner in which you must act or behave is called a
questions.explanation: The manner in which you must act or behave is called the standard of care.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: An adult burn patient with circumferential burns to his right arm, burns to the entire anterior aspect of his right leg, and burns to his genitals will have burns to a total body surface area of
questions.explanation: This patient has burns to 19% of his body surface area. This was calculated by adding 9% for the total right arm, 9% for the anterior aspect of his right leg, and 1% for burns to his genitals.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: The sinoatrial node is located within the
questions.explanation: The sinoatrial (SA) node is located within the right atrium.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Your patient has a pulse rate of 90, a blood pressure of 132/80, and a stroke volume of 70. What is this patient’s cardiac output?
questions.explanation: The patient’s cardiac output is 6,300. The equation for calculating cardiac output is stroke volume multiplied by heart rate. The blood pressure is not necessary for this calculation.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Your patient complains of headache, stiff neck, fever, and has an altered mental status. You should suspect
questions.explanation: If a patient has a headache, altered mental status, and a fever, meningitis should be suspected.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The strength or ability of a pathogen to produce a disease is
questions.explanation: Virulence is the strength or ability of a pathogen to produce a disease.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following is a definitive sign of death?
options: no deep tendon or corneal reflexes
questions.explanation: Dependent lividity is a definitive sign of death.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: If you extend your arm parallel to the ground, this movement is called
questions.explanation: Abduction is motion away from the midline.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The lower airway begins with the
questions.explanation: The lower airway begins with the larynx.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: In an unconscious patient, the most common airway obstruction is caused by
questions.explanation: The most common airway obstruction in the unconscious patient is his or her own tongue.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: A contraindication for inserting an oropharyngeal airway is that a patient
options: is being ventilated with a BVM.
questions.explanation: A patient with an intact gag reflex is a contraindication for inserting an oropharyngeal airway. If your patient does have an intact gag reflex and you choose to use an airway adjunct, insert a nasopharyngeal airway.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Your patient is an apneic child. How should you ventilate this patient?
options: one breath per five to six seconds
one breath per three to five seconds
one breath per five to eight seconds
questions.explanation: An apneic child should be ventilated with one breath every three to five seconds.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The sudden blood loss of _______________ in a 1to 8-year-old can be life threatening.
questions.explanation: The sudden blood loss of 500 cc in a 1to 8-year-old child can be life threatening.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The head accounts for what percentage of body surface area in a pediatric patient?
questions.explanation: The head accounts for 18% of body surface area in a pediatric patient.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Your patient is a conscious, injured male, who is in control of his own actions and refusing care. You can treat this patient legally under
questions.explanation: This patient may not be treated. If an adult patient is alert, oriented, and competent, he has the right to refuse care. You must have proper documentation and inform the patient of the benefits of care and the risks of refusal.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: You have used an EpiPen on a patient and have NOT obtained consent prior to treatment. You could be charged with
questions.explanation: If treatment is rendered without a patient’s consent, it is considered battery on the patient.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following is considered a high priority severe burn?
options: partial thickness burns affecting 15%–30% of total body surface area
full thickness burns affecting 3% of body surface area, excluding hands, face, genitals, and upper airway
superficial burns affecting 40% of total body surface area
questions.explanation: Circumferential burns to any extremity are considered a high priority and require transport to a burn center if available.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: The valve that separates the right atrium from the right ventricle is the
questions.explanation: The tricuspid valve is the valve that is between the right atrium and the right ventricle.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What are the only veins in the body that carry oxygenated blood?
questions.explanation: The only veins in the body that carry oxygenated blood are the pulmonary veins. All other veins carry deoxygenated blood back to the heart.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: If a patient is found lying on his left side, he is found in which position?
questions.explanation: A patient who is lying on his left side is left lateral recumbent.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Your patient has fractured her lower jawbone. This bone is known as the
questions.explanation: The mandible is the lower jawbone.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: A normal heart rate for an adolescent patient is
questions.explanation: A normal heart rate for an adolescent patient is 60 to 100 beats per minute.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Your patient is an elderly female, found on a flat, hard surface. If she has a suspected hip injury, the best device to carry and/or move this patient would be a
questions.explanation: Patients who have a suspected hip or pelvic fracture are typically in a significant amount of pain. A scoop stretcher separates in half and can be placed under a patient with limited movement.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: The spleen is located
questions.explanation: The spleen is located in the upper left quadrant.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: An indication for the insertion of a nasopharyngeal airway would be
options: a patient who will not tolerate an oropharyngeal airway.
severe head injury with blood draining from the nose.
questions.explanation: One of the indications for the insertion of a nasopharyngeal airway is a patient who is unable to tolerate an oropharyngeal airway.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When suctioning a pediatric patient, you should suction
options: until the foreign body is removed.
until the return of spontaneous respirations.
for only ten seconds at a time.
questions.explanation: Pediatric patients should only be suctioned for ten seconds at a time. Suctioning longer than ten seconds can cause hypoxia.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: A nasal cannula will deliver approximately what amount of oxygen to a patient?
questions.explanation: A nasal cannula will deliver approximately 24% to 44% of oxygen to a patient.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What stage of labor begins with full dilation of the cervix?
questions.explanation: The second stage of labor begins with the full dilation of the cervix.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: If a newborn isn’t breathing immediately after birth or suctioning of the mouth and nose,
options: use a neonatal BVM and administer breaths at a rate of 40–60 per minute.
invert the newborn and slap his or her buttocks.
questions.explanation: If a newborn isn’t breathing immediately after birth or suctioning to the mouth and nose, give tactile stimulation by either drying the baby, rubbing the back, or flicking the feet.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Good Samaritan laws protect EMT-Bs from a lawsuit
options: only if proper care is provided.
as long as willful negligence is not proven.
as long as the EMT-B does not act outside his or her scope of practice.
questions.explanation: Good Samaritan laws do not protect you from being sued; they only provide an affirmative defense if you are sued. They will also only protect you when you exercise due care and do not deviate from the standard of care.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Before treatment can be rendered to a patient, you must first
questions.explanation: Consent must be obtained prior to rendering treatment to a patient.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: What would be the appropriate treatment of a partial thickness burn to the back with an 18% body surface area?
options: cover the burned area with a dry, sterile dressing
cover the area with a moistened, sterile dressing
apply antibiotic ointment and cover with a dry, sterile dressing
use a needle to decompress the fluid-filled blisters and apply a moistened, sterile dressing
questions.explanation: Burns greater than 15% body surface area should be covered with a dry, sterile dressing to reduce the amount of body heat that is lost.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: What is the outer layer of the heart?
questions.explanation: The epicardium is the outer layer of the heart.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What blood vessel transports blood from the left ventricle in order to begin systemic circulation?
questions.explanation: The aorta transports blood from the left ventricle out to the systemic circulation.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: After the contraction of the right ventricle, blood enters the
questions.explanation: After the contraction of the right ventricle, blood enters the pulmonary artery to reach the lung.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: A patient who is found lying facedown is in what position?
questions.explanation: A patient who is lying facedown is in the prone position.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The gallbladder is located in the
questions.explanation: The gallbladder is located in the upper right quadrant.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The external visible part of the ear is the
questions.explanation: The external, visible part of the ear is the pinna.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The spinal column is divided into how many sections?
questions.explanation: The spinal column is divided into five sections: cervical, thoracic, lumbar, sacral, and coccyx.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When ventilating an unconscious patient without a spinal injury, you notice that the stomach appears to be distending. You should first
options: reposition the head and apply cricoid pressure.
gently press on the stomach to relieve pressure.
questions.explanation: If the abdomen becomes distended while ventilating an unconscious and unresponsive patient, reposition the airway and apply cricoid pressure to seal off the esophagus while ventilating.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Painful bleeding in late pregnancy that is caused by the placenta prematurely separating from the uterine wall is called
questions.explanation: Painful bleeding late in pregnancy that is caused by the placenta prematurely separating from the uterine wall is called abruptio placenta.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Which of the following is an accurate indicator of oxygenation and ventilation in a pediatric patient?
questions.explanation: The best indicator for assessing the oxygenation and ventilation in a pediatric patient is his or her work of breathing.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What is the most common cause of wheezing in pediatric patients?
questions.explanation: The most common cause for wheezing in the pediatric patient is asthma.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When you and your partner arrive to a medical call that is only five minutes from the hospital, the patient is walking toward the ambulance. What should be your first course of action?
questions.explanation: Regardless of the transport time, always size up a scene upon arrival to ensure your safety and the safety of other personnel.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Who regulates all radio operations in the United States?
questions.explanation: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) regulates all radio communications in the United States.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: A hazardous Class 7 indicates
questions.explanation: A hazardous Class 7 indicates the presence of radioactive materials.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Full thickness burns are characterized by
questions.explanation: Full thickness burns are characterized by a dry, leathery appearance.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: At an MCI, a pediatric patient who is breathing independently at a rate of 12 breaths per minute should be tagged with what color using the Jump-Start system?
questions.explanation: A pediatric patient who is breathing independently at a rate of 12 breaths per minute should be tagged with a red triage tag, due to the decreased respiratory rate.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: What is the best way to immobilize a hip fracture?
options: Place the patient in a position of comfort.
Use a long spine board with padding placed under the knees.
Apply a rigid splint to the lower extremity of the side that is affected.
questions.explanation: The best way to immobilize and splint a hip fracture is to place the patient on a long spine board and place padding under the knees.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: After safety, what is the first priority when rendering treatment to a burn patient?
questions.explanation: After scene safety and BSI, it is important to stop the burning process in the burn patient.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: What is the proper order for securing a patient to a long spine board?
questions.explanation: The proper order of securing a patient to a long spine board is to secure the torso first, followed by the extremities, and finally the head.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: At what point should you assess circulation, sensation, and movement when using a short spine board?
options: after the device is completely secured
both a and b –emt-basic practice exam 5149
questions.explanation: When using a short spine board, circulation, sensation, and movement should be assessed prior to and after the application of the device.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The blood vessels that supply blood to the heart muscle are the
questions.explanation: The blood vessels that supply blood to the myocardium are the coronary arteries.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What arteries supply the head and brain with blood?
questions.explanation: The carotid arteries supply the head and brain with blood.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What allows for the exchange of waste and nutrients at the cellular level?
questions.explanation: Capillaries allow for the exchange of waste and nutrients at the cellular level.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What is a condition of insufficient oxygen that can cause chest pain or discomfort?
questions.explanation: Ischemia is a condition of insufficient oxygen that can cause chest pain or discomfort.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Disorganized quivering of the ventricles is called
questions.explanation: Ventricular fibrillation is disorganized quivering of the ventricles.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Treatment of a patient in cardiogenic shock should include
questions.explanation: Preservation of body heat should be included in the treatment for cardiogenic shock.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The collection of fluid in a body part that is closest to the ground is called
questions.explanation: Dependent edema is the collection of fluid in the body part that is closest to the ground.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The upper section of the sternum is called the
questions.explanation: The upper section of the sternum is called the manubrium.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The depression in which the femoral head fits into is called the
questions.explanation: The acetabulum is the depression in which the femoral head fits.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The small, lower extremity bone that lies on the lateral side of the leg is the
questions.explanation: The fibula is the small, lower extremity bone that lies on the lateral side of the leg.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: A Sellick’s maneuver should be performed only when
options: assisting ventilations on a conscious patient.
the patient being ventilated is unconscious.
used in conjunction with BVM. –emt-basic practice exam 5150
questions.explanation: The Sellick’s maneuver should only be performed on an unconscious and unresponsive patient.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When you arrive at the residence of a patient who has a possible airway obstruction, you notice that the patient is coughing forcefully. Bystanders state that he was chewing a piece of steak, laughed, and then began coughing. Your next action should be to
options: start performing abdominal thrusts (i.e., the Heimlich maneuver).
attempt to remove the object manually.
tell the patient to extend his arms over his head.
monitor the patient closely and encourage him to continue coughing.
questions.explanation: If the patient has a mild airway obstruction, he will be able to cough forcefully. If he is able to move air, talk, or cough, continue to allow him to cough and do not interfere with his own efforts to expel the object.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which type of breathing is characterized by irregular respirations followed by a period of apnea?
questions.explanation: Cheyne-Stokes respirations are characterized by irregular respirations followed by a period of apnea.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: How do you measure the nasopharyngeal airway size needed prior to placement?
options: Measure from the tip of the nose to the angle of the jaw.
Measure from the tip of the nose to the earlobe.
Measure from the corner of the mouth to the angle of the jaw.
Measure from the tip of the nose to the top of the ear.
questions.explanation: To choose the appropriate sized nasopharyngeal airway for a patient, measure from the tip of the nose to the earlobe.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The two sizes of oxygen cylinders that an EMT will primarily use are
questions.explanation: The two sizes of oxygen cylinders that an EMT will primarily use are D and M.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Expiratory grunting in a pediatric patient is indicative of
questions.explanation: Expiratory grunting in a pediatric patient is indicative of inadequate oxygenation.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The third stage of labor is complete when
questions.explanation: The third stage of delivery is complete when the placenta delivers.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: What term is used to describe the number of viable births?
questions.explanation: Para is the term that is used to describe the total number of viable births that a mother has had.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: An umbilical cord that is wrapped around an infant’s neck is called a
questions.explanation: An umbilical cord that is wrapped around an infant’s neck is called a nuchal cord.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: When triaging at an MCI, the maximum number of seconds that should be spent assessing each patient is
questions.explanation: When triaging at an MCI, no more than 30 seconds should be spent assessing each patient.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: What is the common ratio for the span of control in an Incident Command System?
questions.explanation: The common ratio for the span of control in an Incident Command System is 1:5. This means that typically the number of elements or people that can be directly managed by another person is five elements to one director or manager.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: What color is used to identify a medical grade oxygen cylinder?
questions.explanation: The color green is used to identify a medical grade oxygen cylinder.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: You respond to a residence and find an elderly male in cardiac arrest. His caregiver states that she remembers seeing a DNR and is going to look for it. What should you do?
options: Wait for the caregiver to return with the proper documentation before rendering treatment.
Advise the police and have them determine the next step.
questions.explanation: Do not delay CPR if a DNR is not readily available or if there is any question as to its existence.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The bottom quadrant of the diamond-shaped NFPA symbol contains information about any
questions.explanation: The bottom quadrant of the diamondshaped NFPA symbol contains information about any special hazards.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Who is responsible for communicating with other agencies?
questions.explanation: The liaison officer is responsible for communicating with other agencies.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What is the best transport decision for a patient with decompression sickness?
options: a trauma center that is ten minutes away
a facility with a hyperbaric chamber that is 20 minutes away
questions.explanation: A facility with a hyperbaric chamber would be the best choice for the transport of a patient with decompression sickness.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Who should direct the movement of a patient when a spinal injury is suspected?
options: the rescuer who is positioned at the head
the rescuer who is positioned at the feet
questions.explanation: The rescuer who is positioned at the head should direct the movement of a patient when a spinal injury is suspected.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Which of the following is a sign of a superficial local cold injury?
options: loss of feeling and sensation in the injury area
questions.explanation: Loss of feeling and sensation is a sign of a superficial local cold injury.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: What section of the vertebral column is most susceptible to injury?
questions.explanation: The cervical spine is the section of the vertebral column that is the most susceptible to injury.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: A condition where severe force or weight is placed upon the thorax, forcing blood from the right atrium into the circulation of the head and neck, is
questions.explanation: Traumatic asphyxia is a condition where severe force or weight is placed upon the thorax, forcing blood from the right atrium into the circulation of the head and neck.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: If a cold injury appears to be deep, you should NOT
questions.explanation: If a cold injury appears to be deep, you should not reheat the injured part.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: The common dosage for the administration of acetylsalicylic acid in a cardiac emergency is
questions.explanation: The common dosage for the administration of acetylsalicylic acid in a cardiac emergency is 324 mg by mouth (PO).
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Nitroglycerine is supplied in which of the following forms?
questions.explanation: Nitroglycerine is supplied as sublingual tablets, sublingual spray, transdermal patches, and cream or ointment.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The bones that make up the toes are the
questions.explanation: The bones that make up the toes are phalanges.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The fibrous tissues that connect bone to bone are
questions.explanation: Ligaments are the fibrous tissues that connect bone to bone.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which part of the nervous system regulates activities in which there is voluntary control, such as talking, walking, etc.?
questions.explanation: The somatic nervous system regulates the activities in which there is voluntary control.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following is the largest part of the brain?
questions.explanation: The cerebrum is the largest part of the brain.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: A patient suffering from heatstroke will typically have what symptoms?
questions.explanation: A patient suffering from a heatstroke will typically have symptoms of hot, dry, flushed skin.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Your patient has had a previous laryngectomy, is conscious, atraumatic, and has agonal respirations. He is lying supine on the floor. How would you ventilate this patient?
options: Use an adult-sized mask directly over the stoma and ventilate with a BVM, using a hand to seal the patient’s nose and mouth.
Insert an ET tube directly into the stoma and ventilate using a BVM.
Use an infantor child-sized mask, make a seal over the stoma, ventilate with a BVM, and use a hand to seal the patient’s nose and mouth.
questions.explanation: A patient with a stoma should be ventilated by using an infantor child-sized mask, making a seal over the stoma, and ventilating with a BVM while maintaining a seal over the patient’s mouth and nose.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What is the normal tidal volume of the bag of an adult BVM device?
questions.explanation: The normal tidal volume of the bag of an adult BVM device is 1,200–1,600 mL.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What method delivers the highest tidal volume to a patient?
questions.explanation: Mouth-to-mask delivers the highest tidal volume to a patient.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following is a contraindication for an oxygen-powered ventilation device?
questions.explanation: COPD is a contraindication for an oxygenpowered ventilation device.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What is the passive part of breathing?
questions.explanation: Exhalation is the passive part of breathing.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: A pregnant female should be transported
questions.explanation: A pregnant female should be transported left lateral recumbent to keep the baby positioned away from the inferior vena cave.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: When suctioning an infant, do NOT suction for more than
questions.explanation: When suctioning an infant, suction for no more than 5 seconds.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: To suction the nose of a newborn, it is recommended to use a
questions.explanation: A bulb syringe should be used to suction the nose of a newborn.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: A normal respiratory rate for a newborn at rest is
questions.explanation: A normal respiratory rate for a newborn at rest is 30 to 50.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When parking at the scene of a motor vehicle crash, an ambulance should be parked
questions.explanation: When parking at the scene of a motor vehicle crash, the ambulance should be parked at least 100 feet away from the collision.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT part of the major functions of the Incident Command System?
questions.explanation: Staffing is not part of the major functions of the Incident Command System.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: What should be applied to an evisceration?
questions.explanation: A moistened, sterile dressing should be applied to an evisceration.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: If a patient sustains a brain injury in the temporal region, she may develop problems with
questions.explanation: If a patient sustains a brain injury in the temporal region, she may develop problems with memory.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Why is an occlusive dressing applied to a sucking chest wound?
options: to seal the wound, preventing air from going into the chest cavity
questions.explanation: An occlusive dressing is applied to a sucking chest wound to seal it, preventing air from entering the pleural cavity.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: What are the principle signs of hypovolemic shock?
options: normal blood pressure and rapid, weak pulse
low blood pressure and bounding pulse
low blood pressure and normal pulse
low blood pressure and rapid, weak pulse –emt-basic practice exam 5154
questions.explanation: The principle signs of hypovolemic shock are a rapid, weak pulse and low blood pressure.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Your patient is complaining of dyspnea and you notice paradoxical movement upon inspiration and expiration. What does this indicate?
questions.explanation: Paradoxical movement in the chest upon inspiration and respiration is indicative of a flail chest.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following is a sign that a patient is NOT getting an adequate amount of oxygen?
options: The patient has an increased respiratory rate.
The patient states that he feels short of breath.
questions.explanation: If a patient has an increased respiratory rate, it is a sign that the patient is not getting an adequate amount of oxygen.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What is the most common cause of seizures in the pediatric patient?
questions.explanation: The most common cause for seizures in a pediatric patient is fever.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following is a risk factor for SIDS?
options: infant who was recently diagnosed with an upper respiratory infection
questions.explanation: A mother who smokes during pregnancy is a risk factor for Sudden Infant Death Syndrome.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The logistics function of the Incident Command System includes
options: providing support to meet the incident needs.
providing all services to support the incident.
questions.explanation: In the Incident Command System, logistics provides resources and support to meet the incident needs. It also provides all services to support the incident.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Who is the only member of personnel who is authorized to release information to the media after approval by the incident commander is given?
questions.explanation: When the incident commander approves, the only person authorized to release information to the media is the public information officer.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Radio communications on an MCI or multisystem response incident should use
questions.explanation: Clear text should always be used on an MCI or when there are multiple organizations responding to an incident.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: A fracture in which the bone is broken into more than two fragments is a(n)
questions.explanation: A fracture in which the bone is broken into more than two fragments is a comminuted fracture.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Your patient is a young male who appears to have a dislocated patella. How should you splint this injury?
options: straighten the leg, then splint with a rigid splint
questions.explanation: A dislocated patella should be splinted in the position that it is found.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: A traumatic injury caused by fragments will most likely occur in what blast phase?
questions.explanation: A traumatic injury caused by fragments will most likely occur in the secondary blast phase.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: What is most important when assessing for injuries sustained from a fall?
questions.explanation: The height of the fall is the most important of these answer choices when you are assessing injuries from a fall.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: After the administration of nitroglycerine, blood pressure should be taken within ________ minutes after each dose.
questions.explanation: The blood pressure should be taken five minutes after the administration of nitroglycerine.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Which of the following is an absolute contraindication for the administration of nitroglycerine?
questions.explanation: Nitroglycerine is absolutely contraindicated in the presence of a head injury.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The pulse of a patient with a malfunctioning pacemaker will usually have a pulse rate that is
questions.explanation: The pulse of a patient with a malfunctioning pacemaker will usually have a pulse rate that is less than 60.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The most common error that occurs with the operation of an AED is
questions.explanation: The most common error that occurs with the operation of an AED is a dead battery.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Blood occupies what percentage of the cranium?
questions.explanation: Blood supply occupies 10% of the cranium.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: A body temperature of what degree and higher is considered to be abnormal?
questions.explanation: A body temperature of 100.4°F or higher is considered to be abnormal.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What component in the blood contains hemoglobin?
questions.explanation: Red blood cells contain hemoglobin.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: How much blood loss can occur with a femur fracture?
questions.explanation: 1,000 cc to 2,000 cc of blood loss can occur with a femur fracture.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: In the late stages of decompensated shock, the body will have a(n) _______________ in blood pressure and the pulse will _________________.
questions.explanation: In the late stages of decompensated shock, the body will have a decrease in blood pressure and the pulse will decrease.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The type of amnesia where the patient is not able to recall events that have happened after a traumatic injury or event is
questions.explanation: A patient who is unable to recall events that happened after an incident or injury likely has anterograde amnesia.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Your patient is found lying unconscious on the sidewalk. No obvious trauma is noted. How should you open the patient’s airway?
options: using the forefinger and thumb in a crossover motion
questions.explanation: The jaw-thrust maneuver should be used to open an airway if it is unknown if the patient has sustained an injury.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a safety feature of high-pressure oxygen cylinders?
questions.explanation: A high-pressure port is not a safety feature of high-pressure oxygen cylinders.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What causes the high-pitched sound in wheezing?
options: a fall in carbon dioxide levels
questions.explanation: Narrowing of the airways causes the highpitched sound of wheezing.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When assessing breathing, look for
questions.explanation: When assessing breathing, look at the rate, depth, and ease.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: You respond to a child with unknown respiratory problems. You find that the child has a tracheostomy tube and is on a ventilator. The ventilator is malfunctioning. You should
options: disconnect the ventilator and ventilate the child using a BVM connected to a tracheostomy tube.
questions.explanation: If a ventilator malfunctions, disconnect the ventilator and ventilate the patient using a BVM. In this case, the patient has a tracheostomy, so the mask portion should be removed from the BVM and the tracheostomy tube should connect directly to the BVM. Ventilate at the proper rate for the patient’s age.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When assessing circulation in a pediatric patient with a weak pulse, check the __________ pulse if the patient is older than one year.
questions.explanation: When there is a weak pulse found in a pediatric patient, the carotid pulse should be checked for circulation if the patient is 1-year-old or older.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: To mount a car seat in an ambulance, place the car seat
options: in the front seat of the ambulance.
questions.explanation: When a car seat is used, mount it to the stretcher, in order to have access to the patient.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: The scene size-up should include
options: the number of injuries, if known.
the number of law enforcement on the scene.
questions.explanation: When performing a scene size-up, include the number of injuries if they are known.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Upon arrival to an incident, an EMT should report to the
questions.explanation: When arriving on the scene of an incident, the EMT should immediately report to the incident commander for assignment.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: A patient with repetitive questioning after a traffic accident most likely has a
questions.explanation: A patient with repetitive questioning after a traffic accident most likely has a concussion.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What should be the next step in your assessment if a significant mechanism of injury is found?
questions.explanation: If a significant mechanism of injury is found, a rapid trauma assessment should be performed.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: In the mnemonic DCAP-BTLS, what does the letter B stand for?
questions.explanation: In the mnemonic DCAP-BTLS, the B stands for burns.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: When you find a patient facedown in water, you should position yourself
options: None of the above. –emt-basic practice exam 6166
questions.explanation: When you find a patient lying facedown in water, position yourself beside him or her.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Low blood flow to the heart is usually caused by
questions.explanation: Decreased blood flow to the heart is typically caused by atherosclerosis.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The heart has ____________ chambers.
questions.explanation: The heart has four chambers. There are two atria and two ventricles.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Blood from the abdomen, legs, and kidneys is carried through the ________________ into the right atrium.
questions.explanation: Blood from the abdomen, legs, and kidneys is carried through the inferior vena cava into the right atrium.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: What is the approximate blood loss that can occur with a rib fracture?
questions.explanation: The approximate blood loss that can occur with a rib fracture is 125 cc.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: What is the artery that is located in the upper arm and is palpated in infants to determine a pulse?
questions.explanation: To determine the presence of a pulse in an infant, palpate the brachial artery, which is located in the upper arm.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What is the condition when a diver starts acting strangely and out of character after diving too deeply or for too long?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen narcosis is a condition that occurs in divers when they have been diving too deeply or for too long. This condition is characterized by strange behavior that is out of character for the diver.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Hypothermia is defined as a core body temperature below
questions.explanation: Hypothermia is defined as a core body temperature that is below 92°F.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What is the membrane that is attached to the lung surface?
questions.explanation: The membrane that is attached to the surface of the lung is the visceral pleura.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Of the following, which is characterized by rapid, deep respirations that are usually present with strokes or head injuries?
questions.explanation: Central neurogenic hyperventilation is characterized by rapid, deep respirations and is usually present with strokes or head injuries.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The three signs that make up Cushing’s triad are
options: decreased blood pressure, decreased pulse, and abnormal respirations.
increased blood pressure, increased pulse, and abnormal respirations.
decreased blood pressure, increased pulse, and abnormal respirations.
increased blood pressure, decreased pulse, and abnormal respirations. –emt-basic practice exam 6167
questions.explanation: The three signs that make up Cushing’s triad are increased blood pressure, decreased pulse, and abnormal respirations.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The type of asphyxia that can occur when you improperly restrain a patient is
questions.explanation: The type of asphyxia that can occur when you improperly restrain a patient is positional asphyxia.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following is a strong indicator of circulatory status in a child who is less than 3-years-old?
questions.explanation: In a child, the skin is a better indicator of circulatory status than blood pressure, pulse pressure, and respiratory rate.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: An infant who is between 1 month old and 1 year old should have a pulse rate of
questions.explanation: An infant who is between 1 month old and 1 year old should have a pulse rate of 100–160.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What is the best way to determine the proper sized equipment for a pediatric patient?
options: Refer to a pediatric resuscitation tape measure.
questions.explanation: The best way to determine the proper sized equipment for a pediatric patient is to use pediatric resuscitation tape.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The treatment group is responsible for
options: the sorting of patients according to their seriousness and injuries.
obtaining resources for the transport of patients to the appropriate facility.
the establishment of a treatment area, where patients can be treated and collected.
questions.explanation: The treatment group is responsible for the establishment of a treatment area, where patients can be treated and collected.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: How many incident command posts should be established on the scene of an incident?
questions.explanation: Only one command post should be established on the scene of an incident.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: A team that is assigned to a specific task is a
questions.explanation: A team that is assigned a specific task is a unit.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The most important aspect of an MCI is
options: the appropriate number of responding units.
questions.explanation: The most important aspect of any scene is scene safety.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Patients who are not breathing after repositioning their airway should be tagged
questions.explanation: Patients who are not breathing after repositioning their airway should be tagged black.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When someone fractures their tailbone, what section of the spine is involved?
questions.explanation: When a patient fractures his or her tailbone, the section of the spine that is involved is the coccyx.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: In addition to obvious injuries, what information is needed to determine injuries or suspected injuries in a trauma patient?
options: the patient’s account of what happened
the mechanism of injury –emt-basic practice exam 6168
questions.explanation: The mechanism of injury is needed in order to determine or suspect injuries in the trauma patient.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: The ______________ refers to the time from injury to definitive care in which the treatment of a trauma patient’s injuries and shock should occur in order to maximize the chance of survival.
questions.explanation: The golden hour refers to the time from injury to definitive care in which the treatment of injuries and shock should occur. This maximizes the chance of survival in the trauma patient.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Which of the following is an atypical symptom of an acute myocardial infarction?
questions.explanation: An atypical symptom that can be present in an acute myocardial infarction is fatigue.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You arrive on the scene to find an unconscious and unresponsive patient lying supine on the ground with his lower extremities in a puddle. Bystanders state that he was complaining of chest pain prior to falling to the ground. Before defibrillating this patient, you should
options: remove the patient from the puddle.
questions.explanation: To prevent arcing, injury, or death, patients must be removed from standing water prior to defibrillating.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: At a minimum, AEDs should be checked every
questions.explanation: At a minimum, AEDs should be checked every 30 days.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Which of the following is a component of the cardiovascular system?
questions.explanation: Blood is a component of the cardiovascular system.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The structures that connect muscles to bones are
questions.explanation: Tendons connect muscles to bones.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: An injury that is caused by stretching or tearing tendons or ligaments is a
questions.explanation: A sprain is an injury that is caused by the stretching or tearing of tendons or ligaments.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Which of the following is a sign of a tension pneumothorax?
questions.explanation: Tracheal deviation is a sign of a tension pneumothorax.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Your partner has just intubated the patient. You should first listen over the ______________ to confirm tube placement.
questions.explanation: To decrease the chance of delay in ventilation or causing distention of the stomach, listen over the stomach initially, after a patient is intubated.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The oropharynx is the area directly
questions.explanation: The oropharynx is the area that is directly posterior to the mouth.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The smallest branches of the bronchi are the
questions.explanation: Bronchioles are the smallest branches of the bronchi.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When performing chest compressions on an infant, compress the chest
questions.explanation: When performing chest compressions on an infant, compress the chest 3 1 to 2 1 its depth.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Which of the following injuries should raise your index of suspicion of child abuse?
questions.explanation: A grid pattern burn should raise the index of suspicion of child abuse.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Stranger anxiety usually develops when the patient is a(n)
questions.explanation: Stranger anxiety usually develops in the toddler years.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You are called to the residence of a woman who is in labor. Examination of her genitalia shows that she has a limb presentation. You should
options: lightly pull on the exposed limb to assist in the delivery.
cover the limb with a sterile towel and transport the woman immediately.
coach the woman to breathe and push.
insert two fingers into the vagina to relieve pressure on the limb.
questions.explanation: A limb presentation during delivery is a severe emergency. Do not attempt to pull or push on the limb. Cover the limb with a sterile towel and transport immediately.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Your first preparation for the delivery of an infant should be
questions.explanation: Your first preparation for the delivery of an infant should be BSI (body substance isolation) precautions.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The orange section of the Emergency Response Guidebook
options: lists hazardous materials in alphabetical order, based on their name.
lists hazardous materials, based on a specific identification number.
questions.explanation: The orange section of the Emergency Response Guidebook contains all the safety recommendations.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: A teratogenic substance
options: causes damage to a developing fetus.
questions.explanation: A teratogenic substance causes damage to a developing fetus.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: A warm zone is the area
questions.explanation: The area surrounding the contamination zone is the warm zone.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Where should vehicles be parked in relation to a fuel leak or hazardous materials release?
questions.explanation: Vehicles should be parked upwind from a fuel leak or hazardous materials release.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: SAMPLE history, rapid trauma assessment, and baseline vital signs are all components of the
questions.explanation: The SAMPLE history, rapid trauma assessment, and baseline vital signs are all components of the focused history and medical exam.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: If a potentially life-threatening condition is identified during the detailed physical exam, you should
options: return to the initial assessment. –emt-basic practice exam 6170
questions.explanation: If a potentially life-threatening condition is identified during the detailed physical exam, you should return to the initial assessment (ABCs).
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The left and right sides of the heart are separated by the
questions.explanation: The left and right sides of the heart are separated by the septum.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: In addition to the atria and ventricles, ______________ also contain valves to prevent the backflow of blood.
questions.explanation: In addition to the atria and ventricles, veins also contain valves to prevent the backflow of blood.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The electrical impulse is held in the ____________ for a fraction of a second to allow for the filling of the ventricles.
questions.explanation: The electrical impulse is held in the atrioventricular node for a fraction of a second to allow for the filling of the ventricles.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: When the heart’s electrical system is firing in a regular rhythm but the myocardium is not responding, this will be reflected on the monitor as
questions.explanation: When the heart’s electrical system is firing in a regular rhythm but the myocardium is not responding, this will be reflected on the monitor as pulseless electrical activity (PEA).
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: A geriatric patient is considered to be a patient who is ___________ years or older.
questions.explanation: A geriatric patient is considered to be a patient who is 65-years-old or older.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The leaf-shaped structure that covers and prevents food and other foreign matter from entering the trachea is the
questions.explanation: The epiglottis is the leaf-shaped structure that covers and prevents food and other foreign matter from entering the trachea.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The knee is _______________ to the hip.
questions.explanation: The knee is distal to the hip.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When a patient has loose dentures you should ______________ before ventilating.
questions.explanation: When a patient has loose dentures you should remove them prior to ventilating the patient.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When a patient hyperventilates, they have a buildup of ______________ in the blood.
questions.explanation: When a patient hyperventilates, they have a buildup of carbon dioxide in the blood.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What should the oxygen flow rate be set to when using a small volume nebulizer?
questions.explanation: The oxygen flow rate should be set on 6 LPM to 8 LPM when a small volume nebulizer is attached.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: If lung sounds are present on the right side and diminished on the left side, this means that the _______________ has been intubated.
questions.explanation: If lung sounds are present on the right side and diminished on the left, this means that the right mainstem bronchus has been intubated.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What is the BURP maneuver used for?
options: to reduce the potential for vomiting during intubation
to assist in the visualization of the vocal cords during intubation
questions.explanation: The BURP method is used to aid in the visualization of the vocal cords during intubation.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When a mother is in true labor,
options: there will be a brownish-colored bloody show.
pain starts and stays in the lower abdomen.
change in position does not relieve contractions.
questions.explanation: When a mother is in true labor, a change in her position will not relieve the contractions.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: When preparing for the delivery of an infant, you notice that the amniotic sac is protruding from the vagina as the head is crowning. You should
options: support the infant’s head and encourage the mother to push.
apply a moist, sterile pad to the vagina and transport.
questions.explanation: If the amniotic sac is protruding from the vagina when the infant’s head crowns, puncture the sac, aiming away from the infant’s face.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: In addition to the APGAR score, your documentation of the infant should include the
options: names of the personnel on the scene.
questions.explanation: In addition to the APGAR score, the time of birth should be documented.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: To slow bleeding after childbirth,
options: place a sanitary pad on the vagina.
apply direct pressure to the vagina.
questions.explanation: To slow the mother’s bleeding after childbirth, gently massage her abdomen.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: A woman who is experiencing her first pregnancy is called
questions.explanation: Primigravida is the term that is used when a woman is experiencing her first pregnancy.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: To remove the roof of a vehicle with an entrapped victim using a hydraulic-powered cutter, cut through the_________ with a relief cut to the roof and fold the roof over.
questions.explanation: To remove the roof of a vehicle with an entrapped victim using a hydraulic-powered cutter, cut through the A and B posts with a relief cut to the roof. Then, fold the roof over.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: _________________ is the oversight of all patient care aspects in EMS.
questions.explanation: Medical direction is the oversight of all patient care aspects in EMS.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: If you are the initial unit to arrive on the scene of a multicasualty incident, you should first
options: report to the command post for assignment.
questions.explanation: As the initial arriving unit on the scene of an incident, it is your responsibility to establish command.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: A condition in which the artery walls become hard and stiff due to calcium deposits is
questions.explanation: Arteriosclerosis is a condition in which the artery walls become hard and stiff due to calcium deposits.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The dilation or ballooning of a weakened area in the wall of an artery is called
questions.explanation: The dilation or ballooning of a weakened area in the artery wall is called an aneurysm.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Accumulation of fluid in the lungs is called
questions.explanation: Pulmonary edema is the accumulation of fluid in the lungs.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: You arrive on the scene to find a 59-year-old male patient sitting in his living room, complaining of crushing chest pain. He rates the pain 10:10, his skin is pale, and he is diaphoretic. Your next action should be to immediately
questions.explanation: Oxygen should be applied to a patient who is complaining of chest pain.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: In what part of the skin are nerve endings located?
questions.explanation: Nerve endings are located in the dermis.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You arrive to find your patient sitting upright in a chair. This position is called
questions.explanation: The Fowler’s position is a position in which the patient is sitting upright.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The dorsalis pedis artery is located
options: on the posterior surface of the medial malleolus.
on the anterior surface of the foot.
on the lateral aspect of the anterior wrist.
questions.explanation: The dorsalis pedis artery is located on the anterior surface of the foot.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following most closely mimics the signs and symptoms of an acute stroke?
questions.explanation: The signs of hypoglycemia closely mimic those of a CVA.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Your patient is an elderly female who is found lying supine in bed. She is cool to the touch with a decreased body temperature. She suffered heat loss due to
questions.explanation: A patient who is lying in bed and is sheltered by her home most likely loses body heat due to radiation.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Pupils that are constricted are
questions.explanation: Pupils that are constricted are smaller than normal.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What component of blood is responsible for transporting oxygen and carbon dioxide to and from the tissues of the body?
questions.explanation: Red blood cells are responsible for transporting oxygen and carbon dioxide to and from the tissues of the body.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: You have assisted your patient in taking his nitroglycerine. His next set of vital signs indicates that he is now hypotensive, and he is still complaining of chest pain. Your next action should be to
options: administer a second dose of nitroglycerine.
contact medical control for orders. –emt-basic practice exam 6173
questions.explanation: When a patient suddenly becomes hypotensive, lay him flat.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The first stage of labor begins with the
questions.explanation: The first stage of labor begins with the onset of contractions.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: When performing CPR on an infant, deliver_____________ ventilations and _____________ compressions per minute.
questions.explanation: When performing CPR on an infant, deliver 36 ventilations and at least 100 compressions per minute.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What condition should be suspected in all patients with vaginal bleeding?
questions.explanation: Shock should be suspected in all patients with vaginal bleeding.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: You arrive on the scene of an initial call of chest pain. You find the patient unconscious, unresponsive, pulseless, and apneic. Your first action should be to
questions.explanation: In an unwitnessed arrest, chest compressions should be performed for two minutes before applying the AED.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Angina is often triggered by
options: complete blockage of a blood vessel.
questions.explanation: Angina is often triggered by exertion.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: When using an AED after the shock is administered or when no shock is indicated, CPR should be performed for
