questions.explanation: When using an AED after the shock is administered or when no shock is indicated, CPR should be performed for two minutes.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Your patient opens her eyes to the sound of your voice, withdraws from pain, and is confused. Her GCS is
questions.explanation: The Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) of this patient is 11.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT part of the assessment when using the Cincinnati Prehospital Stroke Scale?
questions.explanation: The ability to swallow is not part of the assessment in the Cincinnati Prehospital Stroke Scale.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When a patient is in anaphylaxis, the exaggerated immune response causes blood vessels to _______________ and the airway passages to _______________.
questions.explanation: When a patient is in anaphylaxis, the exaggerated immune response causes blood vessels to dilate and the airway passages to constrict.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What is the dose for activated charcoal for adults and children?
options: 12.5 mg activated charcoal/kg of body weight
1 g activated charcoal/kg of body weight
1 mg activated charcoal/kg body weight –emt-basic practice exam 6174
questions.explanation: The dose for activated charcoal in adults and children is 1 g activated charcoal/kg of body weight.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, which of the following is the third leading cause of death in the 15to 24-year-old age group?
questions.explanation: According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, suicide is the third leading cause of death in the 15to 24-year-old age group.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What is a false belief in which a patient believes he or she is being harmed, followed, or persecuted?
questions.explanation: Paranoia is a false belief in which the patient believes he or she is being harmed, followed, or persecuted.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: After receiving a medication order by medical control, you should
options: check the expiration date of the medication.
verify the proper medication form and dose.
questions.explanation: After receiving a medication order by medical control, verify the proper medication and dose.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Exhaled air contains__________ oxygen.
questions.explanation: Exhaled air contains 16% oxygen.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When the level of carbon dioxide increases in the blood, the impulse to breathe
questions.explanation: The brain stem contains nerves that act as sensors to monitor the level of carbon dioxide in blood. When the level of carbon dioxide becomes too high, impulses are sent from the brain stem and down the spinal cord, causing the diaphragm and abdominal muscles to contract. The higher the level of carbon dioxide, the stronger the impulse. This increases the respiratory rate.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The three primary characteristics that you must evaluate when assessing breath sounds are
questions.explanation: The three primary characteristics that you must evaluate when assessing breath sounds are depth, ease, and rate.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: The scope of practice is most commonly defined by
questions.explanation: State law defines the scope of practice. Your medical director then further defines it by creating and writing your protocols and SOPs.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Upon arrival to the scene of a motor vehicle crash, you should be looking for
options: hazardous and leaking materials from the vehicles.
overhead hazards, such as power lines, trees, etc.
questions.explanation: Upon arriving on the scene of a motor vehicle crash, you should perform the scene sizeup before exiting your vehicle. This should include looking for overhead hazards, such as power lines and trees, looking at the stability of the vehicles involved, and observing for hazardous and leaking materials from the vehicles.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: When a patient is in anaphylaxis, the exaggerated immune response causes blood vessels to _______________ and the airway passages to _______________.
questions.explanation: When a patient is in anaphylaxis, the exaggerated immune response causes blood vessels to dilate and the airway passages to constrict.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Your patient is a 19-year-old male with a severe nosebleed and no other signs of trauma. In which position should you place him?
options: flat on his back, with lower extremities elevated
questions.explanation: Have the patient sit up and lean forward so the blood does not enter the airway or stomach.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Using a reservoir with a bag-valve-mask system will allow oxygen levels to increase to nearly
questions.explanation: The use of a reservoir ensures that the patient receives the highest concentration of oxygen available.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What should you assume when you ear wheezing during respiration?
options: The patient cannot keep the airway open.
There is liquid in the airway.
The patient is using accessory muscles.
questions.explanation: Wheezing indicates that smaller airways and bronchioles are constricted, usually because of infection or allergy. Fluid is only one of the possible reasons; bronchial constriction is another cause.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Egg fertilization normally occurs in the
questions.explanation: Egg fertilization normally occurs in the fallopian tube.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Another medical term for the womb is
questions.explanation: A medical term for the womb is uterus.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: As the infant moves through the birth canal during birth, what structure bulges significantly?
questions.explanation: As the infant moves through the birth canal during birth, the perineum will bulge significantly.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Your patient has a large laceration to the top of his head. This area is best described as
questions.explanation: Superior is the part of the torso or any other part of the body that is close to the head.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: You are dispatched to a residence of a patient who has jumped into the shallow end of a pool headfirst. The scene is safe and the patient is breathing. Your first action should be to
options: remove the patient from the water.
stabilize the patient’s c-spine while the patient is still in the water.
place the patient on a backboard.
questions.explanation: Any patient who has a suspected spinal injury should have his c-spine immobilized while the EMT simultaneously assesses his airway. This includes stabilizing the c-spine prior to moving a patient out of water.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: You are dispatched to the scene of a gunshot wound to the abdomen. You would expect this patient’s skin to be
questions.explanation: A patient who has sustained an open wound to the abdomen will usually have pale skin. This is due to the shunting of blood to the most vital organs.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Newton’s _____________ law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
questions.explanation: Newton’s third law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The energy of a moving object is called
questions.explanation: Kinetic energy is the energy of a moving object.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: What blood vessels supply blood to the heart muscle and are located inferiorly to the aortic valve, starting at the first part of the aorta?
questions.explanation: Coronary arteries, which are located inferiorly to the aortic valve beginning at the first part of the aorta, supply blood to the heart muscle.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The ___________ deliver(s) blood to all the systemic arteries for transport to the tissues of the body.
questions.explanation: The aorta delivers blood to all the systemic arteries for transport to the tissues of the body.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What has been the leading killer of Americans since the 1900s?
questions.explanation: Cardiovascular disease has been the leading killer of Americans since the 1900s.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The heart is divided down the middle into two sides (right and left) by a wall called the
questions.explanation: The heart is divided down the middle into two sides (right and left) by the septum.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The ________________ divides into two major branches, which supply blood to the left ventricle.
questions.explanation: The left coronary artery divides into two major branches, which supply blood to the left ventricle.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You have responded to the scene of a motor vehicle accident and suddenly feel overwhelmed. You should
options: ask bystanders to call 911 and have them tell the dispatcher that you need more help.
tell yourself it is not your emergency and you are there to help.
not show outward signs of anxiety.
take a step back and call for additional resources.
questions.explanation: If you are on a call and start feeling overwhelmed, the safest thing for you to do for yourself and your patient(s) is to take a step back and call for additional resources.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Your patient does not speak English and you do not speak his native language. You should
options: do your best treating the patient without communicating.
ignore the patient’s rambling and treat his injuries or illness the best that you can.
questions.explanation: If there is a language barrier while assessing and treating a patient, make every attempt to get a translator.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You have contacted the ED for medical direction. The physician has ordered you to assist the patient with the administration of his inhaler and administer two puffs. You should
options: shake the inhaler prior to administration.
ask the patient if he has any allergies.
clearly and slowly repeat the orders back to the physician.
questions.explanation: When an order has been received by medical control, clearly and slowly repeat the orders as stated by the physician, prior to administering any treatment modality.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You have been informed by a patient’s caregiver that the patient is infected with HIV. Your partner refuses to administer treatment because of fear of her own infection. If she refuses to provide patient care, what can she be charged with?
questions.explanation: Abandonment is defined as the termination of care without the patient’s consent or making provisions for the continuation of care by transferring him or her to a medical professional with skills at the same or higher level. In this situation, the professional established patient contact, then refused to continue patient care without the patient’s consent.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You are employed as an EMT in a tieredresponse system. After assuring scene safety and BSI, your patient is found lying unconscious and unresponsive. Upon exiting the ambulance, you should first
options: check the patient for a pulse.
request paramedics to be dispatched to your location.
questions.explanation: Part of the scene size-up includes requesting additional units. In this situation, it is in the best interest of the patient to obtain care from an advanced life support unit.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Your patient has suffered from an apparent heart attack. You should transport this patient to
options: a facility that has the capabilities for emergent angioplasty.
the facility that is approved by and stated in your protocols.
a level one cardiac center. –emt-basic practice exam 7186
questions.explanation: Always follow your local protocols.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: When a patient’s breathing is reduced to the point where the oxygen intake is not enough to support life, the result is
questions.explanation: Respiratory failure is when a patient’s breathing is reduced to the point where the oxygen intake is not enough to support life.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a component of BVM?
questions.explanation: Capnography is not a component of BVM.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following is the best indicator for adequate ventilations when using a bag valve mask?
questions.explanation: Although all the answers are correct in some way, the best answer that indicates adequate ventilations administered via BVM is by observing chest rise and fall.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: After receiving a medication order by medical control, you should
options: check the expiration date of the medication.
verify the proper medication form and dose.
questions.explanation: After receiving a medication order by medical control, verify the proper medication form and dose.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Which of the following carries sperm from the testicles to the urethra?
questions.explanation: The vas deferens carry sperm from the testicles to the urethra.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The pancreas is located in the
questions.explanation: The pancreas is located in the upper left quadrant.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: _________________ is the bending of a joint.
questions.explanation: Flexion is the bending of a joint.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The bottom of the foot is referred to as the _____________ surface.
questions.explanation: The bottom of the foot is referred to as the plantar surface.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The coccyx is composed of _______________ vertebrae fused together.
questions.explanation: The coccyx is composed of four or five vertebrae, which are fused together.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The umbilical cord contains ___________ blood vessel(s).
questions.explanation: The umbilical cord contains three blood vessels.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The placental barrier consists of __________ layers of cells and allows for nutrients and waste to be passed between the mother and the fetus.
questions.explanation: The placental barrier consists of two layers of cells and allows for the exchange of nutrients and waste between the fetus and the mother.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The _________________ carries oxygenated blood from the mother to the baby’s heart.
questions.explanation: The umbilical vein carries oxygenated blood from the mother to the baby’s heart.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The ________________ carries deoxygenated blood from the baby’s heart to the mother.
questions.explanation: The umbilical artery carries deoxygenated blood from the baby’s heart to the mother.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: In a frontal impact motor vehicle crash, there are typically three collisions that take place. The second collision is
options: the initial vehicle against another vehicle or object.
the organs against another structure or organ in the body.
the patient against the interior of the vehicle.
questions.explanation: The second collision in a motor vehicle crash is when the patient collides against the interior of the vehicle.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who was involved in a motor vehicle crash. He has injuries to his shoulder and temporal region. By this injury pattern, you would suspect a
questions.explanation: Typically, injuries to the shoulder and temporal region occur during a lateral impact.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: If the velocity of a bullet is doubled, the energy that is available to cause damage is
questions.explanation: If the velocity of a bullet is doubled, the energy that is available to cause damage is quadrupled.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Your concern for potentially serious underlying injuries is called
questions.explanation: Your concern for potentially serious underlying injuries is called an index of suspicion.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: A car that is traveling at a speed of 50 miles per hour when it abruptly stops after colliding with a tree is an example of
questions.explanation: Newton’s first law states that objects at rest tend to stay at rest and objects in motion tend to stay in motion, unless acted upon by an outside force. A car that strikes a stationary object is an example of this law.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The injury potential of a fall is directly related to the
options: height from which the patient fell.
questions.explanation: The injury potential of a fall is directly related to the height from which the patient fell.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: The electrical impulse is slowed to oneor twotenths of a second to allow blood to pass into the ventricles at what part of the heart?
questions.explanation: The electrical impulse is slowed to oneor two-tenths of a second at the AV node to allow blood to pass into the ventricles.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: During physical exertion in the normal functioning heart, the increased need for blood supply is easily obtained by
questions.explanation: In the normally functioning heart, the increased need for blood supply during physical exertion is obtained by the dilation of the coronary arteries.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The ________________ supply blood to the upper extremities.
questions.explanation: The subclavian arteries supply blood to the upper extremities.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The ________________ arteries supply blood to the pelvis, groin, and legs.
questions.explanation: The iliac arteries supply blood to the pelvis, groin, and legs.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: ________________have the thickness of approximately one cell and connect arterioles to venules.
questions.explanation: Capillaries have the thickness of approximately one cell and connect arterioles to venules.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Because of the blockage (partial or complete) of blood flow through the coronary arteries, the heart muscle and other tissues fail to get enough oxygen. This insufficient amount of oxygen is also called
questions.explanation: Insufficient oxygen supply to the heart and tissues is called ischemia.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: ______________ help(s) the blood to clot.
questions.explanation: Platelets are responsible for helping the blood to clot.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When dispatched to a call, what should be your first concern?
questions.explanation: Scene and crew safety are paramount when responding to a call.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: When lifting a patient who is in a sitting or supine position and has no suspected spinal injury, the ___________ should be used.
questions.explanation: The extremity lift should be used when lifting a patient who has no suspected spinal injury and is in a seated position.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT an immunization recommended for EMS personnel?
questions.explanation: Varicella is not a required immunization for EMS personnel.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: A legal statement that states the patient’s wishes regarding his or her own healthcare is called a(n)
questions.explanation: An advance directive is a legal statement that states the patient’s wishes regarding his or her own healthcare.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: What does HIPAA stand for?
options: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
Health Information Portability and Accountability Act
Health Information Providability and Accountability Act
questions.explanation: HIPAA stands for Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: When transmitting a radio report to the receiving facility, you should not include the patient’s
questions.explanation: When transmitting a radio report to the receiving facility, the patient’s name should not be included.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The Ryan White Act requires a hospital to notify your department’s designated officer within ____________ hours of the time a patient is identified with an infectious disease.
questions.explanation: The Ryan White Act requires a hospital to notify the responding unit’s designated department officer within 48 hours of the time a patient is identified with an infectious disease.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: A bag valve mask that is not attached to oxygen delivers oxygen at what percentage?
questions.explanation: A bag valve mask that is not attached to oxygen delivers oxygen at 21%.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: How does the air pressure outside of the body normally compare with the pressure within the thorax?
options: The pressure outside of the body is higher.
The pressure outside of the body is lower.
The pressure varies with the age of the patient.
questions.explanation: The air pressure outside of the body is normally higher than the pressure within the thorax.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: After _____________ minutes without oxygen, cells in the nervous system may die or be permanently damaged.
questions.explanation: After two to four minutes without oxygen, cells in the nervous system may die or be permanently damaged.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Pink, frothy sputum is typically seen with a patient who has
questions.explanation: Pink, frothy sputum is typically seen in a patient who is suffering from pulmonary edema.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: During exhalation, the diaphragm moves
questions.explanation: During exhalation, the diaphragm moves upward.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The passive process in which molecules from an area of higher concentration move to an area of lower concentration is
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the process in which molecules from an area of higher concentration move to an area of lower concentration.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The chemical that is inside alveoli and helps keep the alveoli open is called
questions.explanation: Alveoli contain a chemical called surfactant that helps keep them from collapsing.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The ________________ cavity contains the great vessels.
questions.explanation: The chest cavity (thorax) contains the great vessels.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What separates the chest from the abdominal cavity?
questions.explanation: The diaphragm separates the chest from the abdominal cavity.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What is the major artery that is located in the forearm and is palpable at the wrist along the thumb?
questions.explanation: The radial artery is palpable in the forearm along the thumb side of the wrist.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The gland that is responsible for metabolism regulation is the
questions.explanation: The thyroid gland is responsible for metabolism regulation.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Normal skin is ______________ to the touch.
questions.explanation: Normal skin is dry to the touch.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The amniotic sac normally contains ______________ of fluid.
questions.explanation: The amniotic sac normally contains 500 mL to 1,000 mL of fluid.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: A full-term pregnancy is usually ______________ weeks from the first day of the mother’s last menstrual period.
questions.explanation: A full-term pregnancy is usually 36–40 weeks from the first day of the mother’s last menstrual period.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Deliveries that occur before ________________ weeks gestation are considered premature.
questions.explanation: Deliveries that occur before 36 weeks gestation are considered premature.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The fetus develops inside a membrane called the
questions.explanation: The fetus develops inside a membrane called the amniotic sac.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Your first preparation for the delivery of an infant should be
questions.explanation: Your first preparation for the delivery of an infant is BSI precautions. At a minimum, gloves and eye shielding should be used.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The manner in which a traumatic injury occurs is the
questions.explanation: The manner in which a traumatic injury occurs is the mechanism of injury.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Blood clots form in the body depending on the blood’s ability to clot, blood stasis, and
options: the amount of white blood cells in the blood.
the amount of red blood cells in the blood.
questions.explanation: Blood clots form in the body depending on the blood’s ability to clot, blood stasis, and changes in the vessel wall.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: During emergencies, the ________________ redirects blood away from other organs and sends it to the brain, heart, lungs, and kidneys.
questions.explanation: During emergencies, the autonomic nervous system redirects blood away from the other organs, sending it to the brain, heart, lungs, and kidneys.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The average-sized adult has approximately __________ of blood volume.
questions.explanation: The average-sized adult has approximately six liters of blood volume.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: A condition where a patient lacks one or more of the body’s clotting factors is called
questions.explanation: Hemophilia is a condition where a patient lacks one or more of the body’s clotting factors.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When the aortic valve closes, blood flow
options: slows to allow filling of the atria.
increases in order to travel to the peripheral arteries.
questions.explanation: When the aortic valve closes, blood flow stops.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: _____________ are found in all parts of the body and allow for the exchange of waste and nutrients at the cellular level.
questions.explanation: Capillaries are found in all parts of the body and allow for the exchange of wastes and nutrients at the cellular level.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Blood enters the system of veins starting with the
questions.explanation: Blood enters the system of veins starting with the venules.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: A controllable risk factor for coronary artery disease is
options: family history of atherosclerotic coronary artery disease.
questions.explanation: Elevated blood glucose levels are a controllable risk factor for coronary artery disease.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: A danger zone is an area where individuals can be exposed to hazardous materials, toxic substances, sharp metals, etc. This zone is also referred to as a
questions.explanation: A hot zone, also called a danger zone, is an area where individuals can be exposed to hazardous materials, toxic substances, sharp metals, etc.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You respond to a single-vehicle motor crash. Upon arrival to the scene, you see the car has impacted with a power pole and there are downed power lines lying across the car. You should
options: enter the area carefully and be aware of surrounding hazards.
attempt to access the patient(s), while making sure that you are safely grounded.
instruct the patient(s) to remain in the vehicle until the power is turned off or removed.
approach the car carefully and do not touch the area of the car that the power lines are touching.
questions.explanation: If power lines are lying across a car, you must instruct the individuals to remain in the vehicle until the power is turned off or removed. Do not approach the car until you are directed to by the power company.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: In which of the following situations should you NOT initiate CPR?
options: The patient’s spouse is adamant that you not perform CPR.
The patient has a living will written in his own handwriting, which is signed by the patient but not witnessed.
The patient’s guardian has provided a valid DNR.
Prior to becoming pulseless and apneic, the patient stated—in your presence—that he wanted to die and wants no measures performed to save him. –emt-basic practice exam 7192
questions.explanation: When a valid DNR is presented, CPR should not be initiated.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The removal of a motor vehicle from around a patient is called
questions.explanation: Disentanglement is the removal of a motor vehicle from around a patient.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Removing a patient from a vehicle after a significant crash in one fast, continuous step
options: should be done to remove all individuals who have been entrapped.
should be performed when there is a death of an occupant in the car.
questions.explanation: Rapid extrication should only be performed in certain emergent situations, due to the fact that it may increase the risk of harm to the patient.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You are first on the scene of a patient who is unconscious and not breathing. The only equipment you have on your person is a pocket mask. You provide artificial ventilations with the pocket mask. What concentration of oxygen is the patient receiving with each breath?
questions.explanation: Artificial ventilations that are administered with a pocket mask deliver 16% oxygen with each breath given.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: You are dispatched to a patient who is complaining of shortness of breath. This is also called
questions.explanation: Dyspnea is another term for shortness of breath.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When fluid accumulates in the lungs, it interferes with the gas exchange. This condition is called
questions.explanation: The accumulation of fluid in the lungs is called pulmonary edema. This condition interferes with the gas exchange in the alveoli.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: You are responding to a patient who has overdosed on sedatives. You would expect his respirations to be
questions.explanation: Sedatives will cause respirations to be decreased and shallow.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When inserting a nasopharyngeal airway, the curvature of the device should follow the ____________ of the nose.
questions.explanation: When inserting a nasopharyngeal airway, the curvature of the device should follow the floor of the nose.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: You have inserted a nasopharyngeal airway, and the patient is not tolerant of it. You should
options: insert the device in the other nostril.
questions.explanation: If a patient does not tolerate a nasopharyngeal airway, remove it.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: A yellowish tint to the skin and conjunctiva is called
questions.explanation: A yellow tint to the skin and conjunctiva is called jaundice.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When determining if a patient has a behavioral emergency, you should first
options: have law enforcement respond to place the person in custody.
rule out any medical problems or emergencies.
see what medications the patient is prescribed.
questions.explanation: When determining if a patient has a behavioral emergency, you should first rule out any medical problems or emergencies.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Stable patients should be reevaluated at a minimum of every
questions.explanation: Stable patients should be reevaluated at a minimum of every 15 minutes.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: A head bleed that is characterized by a loss of consciousness, a lucid period, then loss of consciousness again is a(n)
questions.explanation: An epidural bleed is characterized by a loss of consciousness, a lucid period, and the loss of consciousness again.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What kind of shock is caused by severe infections that abnormally dilate blood vessels?
questions.explanation: Septic shock is caused by severe infections that abnormally dilate blood vessels.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: To assist in the delivery of the placenta,
options: massage the mother’s abdomen in a circular motion.
gently pull on the umbilical cord.
tell the mother to push continuously after the infant is born.
do not worry about the delivery of the placenta.
questions.explanation: To assist in the delivery of the placenta, massage the mother’s abdomen over the fundus in a circular motion.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You are assessing a non-gravid female with profuse vaginal bleeding after an unknown injury. She has a tear noted just outside of the vagina. Treatment should include all the following EXCEPT to
options: apply a sterile moist compress to the tear.
questions.explanation: Foreign bodies or impaled objects should be left in place and stabilized with bulky dressings.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: You have just assisted in the delivery of an infant. He has no muscle tone, slow respirations, and a heart rate of 98. His hands and feet are blue. What is this infant’s APGAR score?
questions.explanation: This infant’s APGAR score is three. A = 1, P = 1, G = 0, A = 0, and R = 1.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: In a breech presentation, which part of the infant presents first?
questions.explanation: In a breech presentation, the buttocks present first.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: In a breech presentation, if the mother does not deliver within ten minutes of presentation, what should you do?
options: Promptly transport the mother to the hospital.
Gently massage the mother’s abdomen in a circular motion.
Gently pull on the presented part of the infant.
Lay the mother left lateral recumbent and tell her to keep her knees together. –emt-basic practice exam 7194
questions.explanation: In a breech presentation, if the mother does not deliver with ten minutes of the presentation, promptly transport the mother to the hospital.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who has suffered from a significant fall. He begins to vomit bright, red blood. This is called
questions.explanation: Hematemesis is the vomiting of bright red or dark blood.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You have been dispatched to a patient who has fallen through a pane of glass and has a possible arterial bleed. The minimum BSI precautions you should take are
questions.explanation: When there is a suspicion of severe bleeding, gloves and eye protection should be applied at a minimum.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You have applied the PASG to a patient. You should monitor the vital signs
options: once before application and then again after application.
questions.explanation: When the PASG has been applied to a patient, recheck the patient’s vital signs every 5 minutes.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You would apply the PASG in which of the following conditions?
options: a transport time of 30 minutes or less
to control massive soft tissue bleeding in the lower extremities
questions.explanation: The PASG can be applied on a patient to control massive soft tissue bleeding in the lower extremities.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: One of the earliest signs of cardiogenic shock is
questions.explanation: Anxiety is one of the earliest signs of cardiogenic shock.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Complete cessation of cardiac activity is called
questions.explanation: Complete cessation of cardiac activity is called cardiac arrest.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: A monitor/defibrillator that delivers a shock by sending energy in a flow from negative to positive is
questions.explanation: A monophasic defibrillator delivers a shock by sending energy in a flow from negative to positive.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: During delivery, the mother will experience some bleeding. What amount of bleeding is considered to be excessive?
questions.explanation: Postdelivery, a mother will experience some vaginal bleeding. This bleeding is considered excessive if it is greater than 500 mL.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: A developmental defect in which the infant’s spinal cord or meninges protrudes outside of the vertebrae or body is called
questions.explanation: Spina bifida is a developmental defect in which the infant’s spinal cord or meninges protrudes outside of the vertebrae or body.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You are dispatched to a call of an infant with shortness of breath. Upon assessing the infant, you ascertain that the respiratory rate is 32. This finding is
questions.explanation: A respiratory rate of 32 is normal in an infant.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: A proper-sized BVM for neonatal resuscitation would have a ___________mL reservoir.
questions.explanation: A proper-sized BVM for neonatal resuscitation has a reservoir of 450 mL.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The individual who has overall command of the scene in the field is the
questions.explanation: The incident commander has overall command of the scene in the field.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who is complaining of weakness and syncope. His pulse rate is 42 and his blood pressure is 108/60. His wife states that he has a pacemaker. You should initially suspect that
options: he is having an acute myocardial infarction.
questions.explanation: A patient who has a pacemaker will have a normal pulse rate if the pacemaker is functioning properly.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You have responded to a patient who has had a sudden onset of unconsciousness and unresponsiveness. His family states that he has an Automated Implantable Cardiac Defibrillator (AICD). He has no pulse and is in V-fib. You should
options: defibrillate this patient at the normal voltage.
decrease the voltage and administer a shock.
not defibrillate a patient with an internal defibrillator.
questions.explanation: Regardless of the presence of an AICD, the normal voltage should be used when defibrillating a patient.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What carries blood from the body to the right atrium?
questions.explanation: The vena cava carries blood from the body to the right atrium.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What carries blood from the right ventricle to the lungs for gas exchange?
questions.explanation: The pulmonary artery carries blood from the right ventricle to the lungs for gas exchange.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Arteries become smaller and smaller until they develop into
questions.explanation: Arteries become smaller and smaller until they become arterioles.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: How will you know if the application of the PASG has been effective?
options: The affected limb has been immobilized.
questions.explanation: An indication that the application of the PASG has been effective is an increase in blood pressure.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Select the correct order of treatment for the traumatic injury patient.
questions.explanation: The correct order of treatment for the traumatic injury patient is BSI, airway and breathing, controlling significant bleeding, and then the transport decision.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: You suspect hypovolemic shock in your trauma patient. What can you do to treat this condition?
questions.explanation: To treat hypovolemic shock in the trauma patient, control obvious bleeding.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who has bruising and blue-and-black discoloration to the skin. This is also called a(n)
questions.explanation: Bruising and a black-and-blue discoloration on the skin is also called a contusion.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: A patient who has a compression injury may develop a serious condition in which fluid can leak in the spaces between cells. Blood vessels also become compressed, inhibiting blood flow to the surrounding tissues. This condition is called
questions.explanation: Compartment syndrome is a serious condition in which fluid can leak into the spaces between cells. Blood vessels also become compressed, inhibiting blood flow to the surrounding tissues.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: To determine the responsiveness in a child, you should
options: speak loudly and shake the child.
tap the child on the shoulder while speaking loudly.
questions.explanation: To determine the responsiveness in a child, gently tap the child on the shoulder while speaking loudly.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who is approximately 24-hours postpartum and is complaining of a sudden onset of severe chest pain and shortness of breath. You suspect
questions.explanation: Pulmonary embolism can occur during pregnancy, especially after childbirth. If a female who is postpartum complains of acute chest pain and shortness of breath, suspect a pulmonary embolism.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Painless, bright red blood is usually present with
questions.explanation: Painless, bright red blood is usually present with placenta previa.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The umbilical cord normally contains
questions.explanation: The umbilical cord normally contains two arteries and one vein.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a cause for fetal bradycardia?
questions.explanation: An epidural, hypoxia, and narcotic medications are all causes for fetal bradycardia. Maternal anxiety does not lower the infant’s heart rate.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: If a diabetic patient takes his insulin but does not eat, the condition that he may develop is called
questions.explanation: If a diabetic patient does not eat and takes his insulin, hypoglycemia may develop.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: In the early stage of hypothermia, respirations will be
questions.explanation: In the early stages of hypothermia, respirations will be rapid.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following is the membrane that lies directly over the brain tissue?
questions.explanation: The membrane that lies directly over the brain tissue is the pia mater.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What part of the brain is responsible for coordination?
questions.explanation: The cerebellum is responsible for coordination.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Your patient has been stung by a bee. You notice that the stinger is still visible. How would you remove the stinger?
options: You would stick a piece of tape on the area, then quickly pull it off.
Using a gloved hand, you would attempt to pull the stinger out with your fingers.
You would scrape it out, using a plastic card.
You would not attempt to remove the stinger.
questions.explanation: To remove a bee’s stinger, use a plastic card, scraping over the stinger in a downward motion.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When testing your suction equipment prior to use, you should turn it on and make sure that it is able to generate a vacuum of more than ____________mmHg.
questions.explanation: When testing suction equipment, the unit must generate a vacuum of more than 300 mmHg.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: You are suctioning a patient and are unable to clear the debris from her airway. You should
options: do a blind sweep of the mouth using your fingers.
keep attempting to suction the airway.
roll the patient onto her side and attempt to clear her mouth with your gloved hand.
questions.explanation: If you are unable to clear debris out of the airway while suctioning, roll the patient onto her side and attempt to clear her mouth with your gloved hand.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: While suctioning an adult patient, he continuously produces pink, frothy sputum, and his airway fills as fast as you are able to suction it. In this circumstance, you should suction the patient for ______________ seconds and ventilate him for _______________ minute(s), continuing this pattern until the secretions have been cleared from the airway.
questions.explanation: In this situation, suction the patient for 15 seconds and ventilate him for 2 minutes, continuing the pattern until the secretions have been cleared from the airway.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: To maintain a patent airway in a uninjured patient who is breathing on his own, the patient should be placed in the ___________ position.
questions.explanation: To maintain a patent airway in a patient who is breathing on his own and is uninjured, the patient should be in the recovery position.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What is the name of the safety system for large, compressed gas cylinders, such as those with medical oxygen?
questions.explanation: The name of the safety system for large, compressed gas cylinders is the American Standard Safety System.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Of the following injuries, which should receive a yellow tag when triaged?
options: back injury with possible spinal cord damage
questions.explanation: Answer choices a and c should receive a red tag; answer choice d receives a green tag.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: When triaging at an MCI, which of the following should receive a red tag?
options: second degree burns to the anterior chest
questions.explanation: Patients with signs of shock should receive a red (immediate) tag.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Anyone who leaves a hazard zone (hot zone) must
options: remove their clothes prior to leaving.
obtain permission from the incident commander.
questions.explanation: Anyone who leaves a hot (danger) zone must pass through the decontamination area.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You are assessing a minor who has fallen off her bike and has obvious deformity to her upper extremity. She needs to be evaluated at the hospital, but her parents are not available. You are able to treat and transport this patient under
questions.explanation: A minor patient who needs emergency care but has no parent or guardian present can be treated under implied consent.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The best treatment for ventricular fibrillation is
questions.explanation: The best treatment for ventricular fibrillation is early defibrillation.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The initial cardiac electrical impulse in the normal functioning heart originates in the
questions.explanation: The initial electrical impulse in the normal functioning heart originates in the sinoatrial node.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The apex of the heart is located in the
questions.explanation: The apex of the heart is located in the left side of the chest.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Red blood cells are also called
questions.explanation: Red blood cells are also called erythrocytes.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who is complaining of chest pain by obtaining a set of vital signs. The top number of the blood pressure is caused by
questions.explanation: The top number of the blood pressure, the systolic pressure, is caused by the contraction of the left ventricle.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What should be your primary concern on the scene with a burn patient?
options: percentage of the body surface area that is burned
questions.explanation: The primary concern on the scene with a burn patient is scene safety.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: In a patient with an arterial bleed, what is the order in which you should attempt to stop the severe bleeding?
questions.explanation: The correct order to treat an arterial bleed should be direct pressure, elevation, compression of the pressure point, and then tourniquet as last resort and if your protocols permit.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Your patient has fallen off a bike and has abrasions to his right knee. Which type of bleeding corresponds to this injury?
questions.explanation: Bleeding from an abrasion is usually from the capillaries.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a function of the skin?
questions.explanation: Fluid balance, temperature regulation, and protection are all functions of the skin.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What layer of the skin is comprised mostly of fatty tissue?
questions.explanation: The subcutaneous layer is comprised mostly of fatty tissue.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The APGAR score after one minute should be
questions.explanation: The APGAR score after one minute should be seven or greater.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: A woman’s gestation is
questions.explanation: A woman’s gestation is 40 weeks.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The disc-shaped, temporary organ that delivers nutrients and oxygen from the mother to the fetus is the
questions.explanation: The disc-shaped, temporary organ that delivers nutrients and oxygen from the mother to the fetus is the placenta.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When birth is imminent, the mother should be placed
options: supine with her knees up and spread apart.
questions.explanation: When birth is imminent, the mother should be placed supine or in semi-Fowler’s with her knees up and spread apart.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: After week eight, the embryo is now called a
questions.explanation: After week eight, the embryo is called a fetus.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The spitting up of blood is called
questions.explanation: The spitting up of blood is called hemoptysis.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The heel bone is also called the
questions.explanation: The heel bone is also called the calcaneus.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: To check for a carotid pulse, place your fingers _______________ to the larynx.
questions.explanation: To check for a carotid pulse, place your fingers lateral to the larynx.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: A sphygmomanometer is used to
questions.explanation: A sphygmomanometer is used to take blood pressure.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: How many bones are in the vertebral column?
questions.explanation: There are 33 bones in the vertebral column.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The pressure of gas in a full oxygen cylinder is approximately _________ psi.
questions.explanation: The pressure of gas in a full oxygen cylinder is approximately 2,000 psi.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Your patient is an infant who is apneic with a pulse. This patient should be ventilated with __________ breath(s) every ______________ seconds.
questions.explanation: An infant that is apneic should be ventilated with one breath every three seconds.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a component of a BVM?
questions.explanation: A rebreathing valve is not a component of a BVM.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following is true regarding the Sellick’s maneuver?
options: The Sellick’s maneuver should be used to keep the gastric contents from entering the trachea in a patient who is actively vomiting.
The Sellick’s maneuver is used to prevent gastric distention.
Effective ventilation can occur when one rescuer ventilates using a BVM with one hand and applies the cricoid pressure with the other hand.
The Sellick’s maneuver is performed by placing the thumb and index finger on either side of the cricoid cartilage at the superior border of the larynx.
questions.explanation: The Sellick’s maneuver is used to prevent gastric distention.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following is an indication that the artificial ventilation is inadequate?
options: The heart rate does not return to the normal range.
There is adequate chest rise and fall.
The heart rate returns to the normal range.
The ventilations are administered at a proper rate for the patient’s age.
questions.explanation: If the heart rate of a patient who is receiving artificial ventilation does not return to the normal range, the ventilations are inadequate.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When on the scene with a family member of a patient who has died, what should you say?
options: “God has a plan for all of us.”
questions.explanation: When someone has lost a family member, keep your comments sincere and understanding. Do not be judgmental, trite, or offer religious reassurances.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Every patient encounter should be considered
questions.explanation: Every patient encounter should be considered potentially dangerous.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: If you see the potential for a possible violent encounter during a scene size-up, you should
options: find something to use as a weapon.
questions.explanation: If you see the potential for a possibly violent encounter during a scene size-up, call for additional resources.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: The failure to provide the same standard of care that a person with similar training would provide is called
questions.explanation: The failure to provide the same standard of care that a person with similar training would provide is called negligence.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Before you obtain a signature from a patient for refusal of care, you should
options: be sure that the patient is mentally competent.
take at least three sets of vital signs.
questions.explanation: Before obtaining a signature from a patient who is refusing care, be sure that the patient is mentally competent.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Nitroglycerine is a(n)
questions.explanation: Nitroglycerine is a vasodilator.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: When performing CPR on an adult patient, how deeply should the chest be depressed?
questions.explanation: When performing CPR on an adult patient, compress the chest 12 1 to 2 inches.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You have responded to a residence and find that the spouse is doing CPR. She tells you that she has been doing CPR for approximately five minutes. You should first
options: stop compressions and check for a pulse.
ask if the patient has a DNR.
questions.explanation: When arriving on the scene to find a patient who has CPR in progress, stop the compressions and check for a pulse.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You are dispatched to the park basketball courts for a patient who is complaining of severe, crushing chest pain. Upon arrival, you find the patient unresponsive. Your first action should be to
questions.explanation: When assessing an unresponsive patient, open and assess the airway first.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: One of the greatest risks that medical personnel can face when dealing with a traumatic injury patient is
questions.explanation: One of the greatest risks when dealing with a traumatic injury patient is the exposure to blood.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: The type of injury that is characterized by the underlying tissues being forcibly torn away is called a(n)
questions.explanation: The type of injury that is characterized by the underlying tissues being forcibly torn away is called an avulsion.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: The type of injury that is caused when a part of the body is trapped between two surfaces is a(n)
questions.explanation: A crush injury is an injury that is caused when a part of the body is trapped between two surfaces.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: The most serious concern with an electrical burn is
options: the amount of electricity the patient came in contact with.
questions.explanation: The most serious concern with an electrical burn is cardiac arrest or dysrhythmia.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You are treating a patient with severe arterial bleeding to the right forearm, which has not slowed with direct pressure and elevation. After applying a pressure dressing, at what pressure point would you apply pressure?
questions.explanation: If a patient has severe bleeding to the forearm and the bleeding isn’t controlled with direct pressure and elevation, compress the brachial artery.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: You and your partner have just delivered a neonate. After warming, drying, suctioning, and administering tactile stimulation to the baby, she is still not breathing. Your next action should be
questions.explanation: If an infant is not breathing after warming, drying, and tactile stimulation, administer oxygen.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: When a baby is born, it can take up to ____________ for him or her to begin to breathe on his or her own.
questions.explanation: When a baby is born, it can take up to 30 seconds for him or her to begin to breathe on his or her own.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You are dispatched to the local park for a pregnant female who is complaining of severe shortness of breath after being stung by a bee. She tells you that she carries an EpiPen and has a history of allergies to bee stings. You should
options: contact medical control prior to assisting with the EpiPen.
questions.explanation: In this situation, contact medical control prior to the administration of the medication.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You respond to the elementary school for an 8-year-old girl with an altered LOC. The school nurse tells you that the patient has been acting strangely and may have a behavioral emergency. You should first suspect
options: that she is most likely having a diabetic emergency.
that she has not taken her psychiatric meds regularly.
that she is faking it and wants to get out of taking a test.
questions.explanation: Suspect and rule out medical conditions in a patient who is behaving strangely.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Pediatric patients are able to maintain a normal blood pressure until they lose approximately _______________ of their blood volume.
questions.explanation: Pediatric patients are able to maintain a normal blood pressure until they lose approximately one-half of their blood volume.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Another name for the shoulder blade is the
questions.explanation: Another name for the shoulder blade is the scapula.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The gallbladder is located in the
questions.explanation: The gallbladder is located in the upper right quadrant.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The elbow is ______________ to the shoulder.
questions.explanation: The elbow is distal to the shoulder.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: A patient who weighs 130 pounds weighs approximately ___________ kilograms.
questions.explanation: A patient who weighs 130 pounds weighs approximately 59 kilograms.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The central nervous system consists of the
questions.explanation: The central nervous system consists of the brain and spinal cord.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following is true regarding gastric distention?
options: Gastric distention most commonly affects children.
Gastric distention can significantly reduce lung volume.
Fast, gentle breaths should be administered to avoid gastric distention.
In all patients, if you notice that gastric distention is occurring while administering ventilations, reposition the head and apply cricoid pressure.
questions.explanation: Gastric distention can significantly reduce lung volume.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: You may encounter patients who have had their larynx removed. This procedure is called a
questions.explanation: A surgical procedure that removes the larynx is a laryngectomy.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You have responded to a patient who has choked on an unknown object. He is awake and alert but is not moving air. The most effective way to dislodge the foreign body from his airway is to
options: lay the patient on the floor and perform chest thrusts.
use Magill forceps to remove the object.
questions.explanation: The most effective way to dislodge a foreign body in a choking patient who is conscious, awake, and alert is to perform abdominal thrusts.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following statements is true?
options: It is easier to provide ventilations when using a bag-valve-mask device or the mouth-to-mask technique when dentures are left in place.
Dentures should be removed when providing ventilations.
Tight-fitting dentures make it more difficult to obtain a proper face-to-mask seal.
Dentures will cause an inadequate tidal volume to be administered when ventilating a patient.
questions.explanation: It is easier to provide ventilations and maintain a good seal when using a bag-valvemask device or the mouth-to-mask technique when dentures are left in place.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The most difficult aspect of providing artificial ventilations with a BVM is
options: administering the proper tidal volume for the patient.
maintaining a seal on the patient’s face.
questions.explanation: The most difficult aspect of providing artificial ventilations with a bag-valve-mask device is maintaining a seal on the patient’s face.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Once your ambulance has responded to a call,
options: you have a duty to act.
you can cancel only if another responder is on the scene.
questions.explanation: Once your ambulance has responded to a call, a duty to act has been established.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Threatening to forcibly restrain a mentally competent patient in order to transport him could be considered
questions.explanation: Threatening to forcibly restrain a mentally competent patient in order to transport him could be considered assault.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When you are in doubt of the validity of a living will or DNR,
options: you have an obligation to resuscitate.
questions.explanation: If any doubt exists to the validity of a DNR, CPR must be initiated.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Which of the following is a presumptive sign of death?
questions.explanation: The lack of a systolic blood pressure is a presumptive sign of death.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a criterion for proving negligence?
questions.explanation: Abandonment is not a criterion for proving negligence.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following indicates the need for immediate defibrillation?
questions.explanation: Pulseless ventricular tachycardia is an indication for immediate defibrillation.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You are on the scene with a patient who is complaining of chest pain. Nitroglycerine is indicated because
options: he states that he has taken Viagra four days ago.
his pain has increased from three to five on a one to ten scale.
questions.explanation: None of the answers listed are contraindications for the administration of nitroglycerine. Each choice is an indication for the administration of nitroglycerine.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The heart is covered by a fibrous sac called the
questions.explanation: The heart is covered by a fibrous sac called the pericardium.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who has gone into cardiac arrest while still in his vehicle. You should
options: attempt to perform CPR the best that you can.
questions.explanation: A patient who has gone into cardiac arrest while in a vehicle is an indication for rapid extrication.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The medical term for a heart attack is
questions.explanation: The medical term for a heart attack is myocardial infarction.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Normal skin should feel
questions.explanation: Normal skin should feel warm to the touch.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: A patient in a postictal state is one who has
options: stopped breathing but not yet died.
demonstrated the early signs of shock.
had a seizure and is now unresponsive.
just given birth but not yet delivered the placenta.
questions.explanation: A patient who is in a postictal state has just had a seizure and appears to be asleep.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who has been involved in a single vehicle accident. The patient was unrestrained and you notice starring of the windshield. When assessing his pupils, you shine a light into his right eye. Normally, the pupil in the other eye will
options: have the opposite reaction of the right eye.
questions.explanation: When a light is shined into one pupil, normally the other pupil should have the same reaction.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Your patient has an impaled object in the thigh. You should
options: stabilize the object with a bulky dressing.
remove the object, then apply direct pressure.
remove the object, apply direct pressure, and then apply a pressure dressing.
questions.explanation: Impaled objects should be stabilized with bulky dressings.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You are called to the residence of a patient who is complaining of abdominal pain. He tells you that he took a hard hit while playing football two nights ago. Upon examination, you find signs of internal bleeding. You should suspect
questions.explanation: A delayed rupture of the spleen can take hours or days for symptoms to develop.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: A blood loss of _____________ in an infant can be life threatening.
questions.explanation: A blood loss of 100 cc to 200 cc can be life threatening in an infant.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: In an infant, what may bulging fontanels indicate?
questions.explanation: Bulging fontanels in an infant can mean an increase in intracranial pressure.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Infants primarily breathe through
questions.explanation: Infants primarily breathe through their noses.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Which of the following is true regarding intercostals muscles of children?
options: They are used frequently in normal breathing.
The muscles are immature, causing fatigue rather quickly.
They are rarely used in respiratory distress.
questions.explanation: Children can get respiratory fatigue rather quickly because their intercostals muscles are immature.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Since blood pressures are difficult to obtain in an infant or child less than 3 years old, your assessment of circulation must rely on and include the capillary refill, quality of pulses, and
questions.explanation: Since blood pressures are difficult to obtain in infants and children less than 3-years-old, you must rely on the capillary refill, quality of pulses, and mental status to assess their circulation.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: A normal blood glucose level for an adult is
questions.explanation: A normal blood glucose level for an adult is 80 mg/dL to 120 mg/dL.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Reddened skin is called
questions.explanation: Reddened skin is called erythema.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When referring to a medication, the ________________ name is the name that a pharmaceutical company gives to the medication.
questions.explanation: The trade name of a medication is the name that the pharmaceutical company gives to it.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following medications is supplied as a suspension?
questions.explanation: Activated charcoal is supplied as a suspension.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What is the lowest possible score given on the Glasgow Coma Score?
questions.explanation: The lowest possible score given on the Glasgow Coma Scale is three.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Once oxygen-poor blood passes through the capillaries, it moves to the
questions.explanation: Once oxygen-poor blood passes through the capillaries, it moves to the venules.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Breathing concentrations of carbon dioxide greater than _________ will result in death within minutes.
questions.explanation: Breathing concentrations of carbon dioxide greater than 10% will result in death within minutes.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Another term for whooping cough is
questions.explanation: Another term for whooping cough is pertussis.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The space between the base of the tongue and the epiglottis is known as the
questions.explanation: The space between the base of the tongue and the epiglottis is known as the vallecula.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The last portion of tracheal cartilage that separates the opening into the right and left mainstem bronchi is the
questions.explanation: The carina is the last portion of the tracheal cartilage that separates the opening into the right and left mainstem bronchi.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT an aim of HIPAA?
options: improving the continuity of health insurance coverage
preventing healthcare workers from sharing patient information
strengthening laws for the protection of the privacy of health information –EMT-Basic Practice Exam 8217
questions.explanation: HIPAA aims to improve the continuity of health insurance coverage, safeguard patient confidentiality, and strengthen laws for the protection of the privacy of health information.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: You are dispatched to respond to a patient who has burns to his chest. Your top priority should be
options: to ascertain the source of the burn.
if there is a need for additional units.
questions.explanation: The top priority when responding to a call is safety.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who has been ejected from a car. He is breathing four times per minute and has an open fracture to his right femur. What should you do first?
options: Stop the bleeding from the femur fracture and apply a splint.
Apply high flow O2 via a nonrebreathing mask.
questions.explanation: Assisting ventilations with a bag-valve-mask is your first priority with this patient.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: If the scene is NOT safe,
questions.explanation: If the scene is not safe, do not enter it.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: The agency that develops the National Standard Curricula for various levels of providers is the
options: National Registry of Emergency Medical Technicians.
National Association of Emergency Medical Technicians.
National Association of Prehospital Medical Providers.
questions.explanation: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration develops the National Standard Curricula for various levels of providers.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The orbit (eye socket) is made up of what bones?
questions.explanation: The orbit is made up of the frontal, zygomatic, and maxillae bones.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The strongest bone in the body is the
questions.explanation: The strongest bone in the body is the femur.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: The assessment of the airway should take no longer than
questions.explanation: The assessment of the airway should be done rapidly and completed within 15 seconds.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who is unconscious and unresponsive. You notice that he has agonal respirations. You have determined this because agonal respirations are characterized by
questions.explanation: Agonal respirations are considered dying gasps and are characterized by irregular, occasional gasps for air.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Normal breathing has which of the following characteristics?
questions.explanation: Normal breathing will have a regular rise and fall of the chest, movement of the abdomen, and a normal rate and rhythm.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: In compensated shock, the respirations will be
questions.explanation: During compensated shock, the body will be able to compensate for the drop in blood pressure. During compensated shock, respirations will increase.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: Which of the following is the narrowest part of the airway in an adult?
questions.explanation: The glottis opening is the narrowest portion of the adult airway.
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: What is another word for the cheek bone?
questions.explanation: The zygomatic bone is the cheek bone.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What is the largest organ of the body?
questions.explanation: The skin is the largest organ in the body.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: When should the PASG be removed in the field?
questions.explanation: The PASG, or MAST pants, should never be removed in the field. PASG should only be removed under the supervision of a physician and preparations should be made for a dramatic drop in blood pressure.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What structure is also called the Adam’s apple?
questions.explanation: The thyroid cartilage makes up what is known as the Adam’s apple.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You are assessing a patient who has overdosed on cocaine. You would expect his pupils to be
questions.explanation: Cocaine overdoses will present with pinpoint pupils, unless other drugs have been taken along with cocaine.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following is more emergent?
options: a child who is not crying and has poor muscle tone and pale skin
a screaming child with a laceration to the back of the head
a child with a humeral fracture and bruising to the face
a whimpering child who is afraid of you –emt-basic practice exam 9228
questions.explanation: A child who is not crying and has poor muscle tone and pale skin is presenting in an emergent condition. The child should immediately be evaluated for life-threatening problems.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: In infants, most of their body heat is lost through their
questions.explanation: Infants lose the majority of their body heat through their heads.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You are dispatched to a residence for a pediatric patient in respiratory distress. The patient’s father states that the child had fallen into the pool earlier that day but was released from the hospital after a brief evaluation. He is most likely suffering from
questions.explanation: Secondary drowning syndrome can occur within 24 hours after a near-drowning incident.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You are assessing a pediatric patient with special needs. His caregiver states that he has a history of hydrocephalus. You notice a bulge to the side of his head and feel something hard under the surface of the scalp. This is probably due to the presence of a
questions.explanation: A ventriculoperitoneal shunt is placed in the brain to help drain excess CSF and would be common in a child with hydrocephalus.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: The birth of the infant is in what stage of labor?
questions.explanation: The birth of an infant occurs in the end of the second stage of labor.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Which is NOT a required element for an accusation of negligence to hold up in the court of law?
questions.explanation: All the answers listed are required elements for a negligence lawsuit to hold up in court.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: On a hazardous materials call, park your ambulance
questions.explanation: At the scene of a hazardous material call, park the ambulance uphill and upwind in order to lessen the exposure to toxic fumes.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: A competent adult patient who gives permission to treat does so with
questions.explanation: A competent adult gives permission to treat with expressed consent.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The first incident management system was known as the
questions.explanation: The first incident management system was originally known as the incident command system (ICS).
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Which of the following is considered a member of the incident management system’s command staff?
questions.explanation: The safety officer reports directly to the incident commander and is considered a member of the command staff.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: A significant finding in head trauma that indicates a skull fracture is
options: a full thickness laceration with bone exposure. –emt-basic practice exam 9229
questions.explanation: The presence of cerebrospinal fluid should always raise suspicion for a skull fracture.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: You are dispatched to a minor MVC. The patient, sitting on the curb side, tells you that he was just bitten by ants and has severe allergies to many insect bites. His skin is currently warm and dry, and he doesn’t appear to be in distress. You should
