Her mother states that she found the child playing in the bathroom. A flavored children’s aspirin bottle was found nearby with only a few tablets in it. Children’s aspirin is in which of the following classes of medications?
questions.explanation: Salicylates is the correct class of drugs for aspirin.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You respond to a 4-year-old female who has taken an unknown quantity of children’s aspirin. Upon arrival, you find the patient conscious, crying, and lethargic. Her mother states that she found the child playing in the bathroom. A flavored children’s aspirin bottle was found nearby with only a few tablets in it. Correct treatment for this patient may include
options: IV, oxygen, naloxone, and NG tube -insertion.
questions.explanation: The appropriate treatment is IV, oxygen, ECG, and activated charcoal. Be prepared to treat dysrhythmias and to provide a fluid challenge if ordered. Sodium bicarbonate may also be ordered by medical direction, but epinephrine is not indicted for this situation. Naloxone is not indicated in this overdose situation, but could always be given if you are unsure of the substances ingested.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: A 52-year-old female is complaining of chest tightness with radiation to the back. Her EKG shows a sinus tachycardia with multifocal premature ventricular contractions occurring ten times per minute. Her blood pressure is 112/68, and her heart rate is 96 and irregular. Which of the following medications would be indicated for her condition?
questions.explanation: It is rare to treat PVCs directly; the administration of oxygen and nitrates would be used to reperfuse the myocardial tissue, which in turn may eliminate the PVCs. However, in this case, she is exhibiting more than six per minute, and they are originating from more than one site. Lidocaine may be needed to control these potentially malignant ectopic beats.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Children of which age group are in the greatest danger from airway obstruction caused by aspirated foreign objects?
questions.explanation: Children between 1 and 3, who tend to put things into their mouths, are in greatest danger of aspiration of foreign objects.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: What is the usual pediatric dose of naloxone for a child less than 5 years old?
questions.explanation: The pediatric dosage is 0.1mg/kg for a child less than 5 and 2.0 mg for a child over 5 years of age.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You are called to the scene of a possible drowning at a local pool. Upon arrival, you discover that lifeguards have removed the patient from the pool and are performing rescue ventilations since the patient is apneic with a pulse. Upon placing the monitor, you discover a rapid rhythm at 200 beats per minute, no P waves, and a QRS complex of 0.16. Management should consist of
questions.explanation: This patient presents in pulseless ventricular tachycardia, a lethal rhythm. Immediate defibrillation is indicated to terminate this event.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You must perform chest compressions on a newborn infant if, after oxygenation and ventilation, the heart rate persists in being less than what number?
questions.explanation: The threshold for bradycardia in a newborn infant is 80 beats per minute, and the range where you would consider the need for compressions along with other treatments is between 60 and 80 BPM. Any infant with a heart rate less the 60 BPM should immediately receive compressions, but if the rate remains between 60–80 and is not rapidly increasing despite positive pressure ventilation with 100% oxygen, you should perform chest compressions for 30 seconds, reassess, and repeat as needed.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Your patient is age 7 and has a suspected broken arm and numerous bruises. The mother states that the child was hurt when he fell off his bike in the morning. What finding would lead you to suspect that the mother’s account of the injuries is not true?
options: multiple fresh bruises on the child’s arm and leg
the child cries from pain when you palpate
the child clings to the mother during -transport
questions.explanation: Although children are often uncoordinated and subject to frequent falls, the presence of bruises in various stages of healing would lead you to suspect that this child had been injured on more than one occasion. Carefully note the environment the child is living in and report any -suspicions of abuse or neglect to the proper authorities.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You respond to a 25-year-old female who is complaining of vaginal bleeding and abdominal pain. The patient states that she is 33 weeks pregnant and that this is her first pregnancy. She says that when the pain started, it felt like “something was tearing.” She denies vaginal bleeding during the pregnancy prior to this event. Upon assessment, you notice what appears to be approximately 500 cc of dark, almost black, blood. Which condition is this patient most likely suffering from?
questions.explanation: The tearing feeling and the dark-colored blood are classic signs and symptoms of abruptio placenta. Placenta previa often has bleeding that is contained within the uterus due to the placenta blocking the os (opening or mouth) of the uterus.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You respond to a 25-year-old female who is complaining of vaginal bleeding and abdominal pain. The patient states that she is 33 weeks pregnant and that this is her first pregnancy. She says that when the pain started, it felt like “something was tearing.” She denies vaginal bleeding during the pregnancy prior to this event. Upon assessment, you notice what appears to be approximately 500 cc of dark, almost black, blood. This condition is dangerous to the life of
questions.explanation: Both lives are at stake. Oxygen is passed from the mother to the baby via the placenta. A separation greatly decreases the blood supply to the infant and the uncontrolled bleeding is dangerous to the mother.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You respond to a 25-year-old female who is complaining of vaginal bleeding and abdominal pain. The patient states that she is 33 weeks pregnant and that this is her first pregnancy. She says that when the pain started, it felt like “something was tearing.” She denies vaginal bleeding during the pregnancy prior to this event. Upon assessment, you notice what appears to be approximately 500 cc of dark, almost black, blood. In addition to high-flow oxygen and continuous monitoring of the mother’s vital signs and the baby’s fetal heart tones, you should treat this patient with
options: one or two large-bore IVs of normal saline or Ringer’s lactate.
one minidrip IV of normal saline and pitocin or terbutaline.
one minidrip IV and PASG with all compartments inflated.
Basic life support treatments are all that are needed.
questions.explanation: Administering one or two large-bore IVs of normal saline or Ringer’s lactate is appropriate when combined with rapid transport. You should titrate the BP to 100–110 so as not to overload the circulation, causing pulmonary edema and further compromising the oxygen supply to the infant.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Patients with pelvic inflammatory disease often complain of which of the following?
questions.explanation: The most common presentation of PID is diffuse, moderate to severe lower-abdominal pain, which makes ambulation difficult.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: The two primary goals of prehospital care of a sexual assault victim are to preserve the victim’s privacy and dignity and to
options: obtain a complete description of the assault.
collect samples of body fluids from the patient.
preserve all physical evidence for the police.
help the victim bathe and change her -clothing.
questions.explanation: In order to preserve physical evidence of the assault, avoid cleaning wounds and do not allow the victim to bathe or change her clothing. If clothing is removed during patient care, it should be placed in a brown paper bag and handed over to police officers. Maintain chain of custody carefully to preserve the evidence.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: A 24-year-old female is complaining of chest pain and difficulty breathing. She has been up for three days studying for finals and has been taking ephedrine supplements to help her stay awake and alert. She also admits to drinking 12 caffeinated soft drinks in the past day. Vitals are BP 80/40, P 180 carotid, and R 42. She is very pale and lethargic. The ECG shows a narrow QRS complex with regular R-R intervals, with no discernable P or T waves at 200 beats per minute. The best treatment for this patient would include
questions.explanation: This patient presents in unstable supraventricular tachycardia. Her condition may deteriorate quickly; therefore, immediate synchronized cardioversion is indicated.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What does it mean if a woman is described as multipara?
options: She is pregnant and morbidly obese.
She has delivered more than one baby.
She has never delivered a viable infant.
She is over 45 years old and pregnant.
questions.explanation: A multipara is a woman who has delivered more than one live baby.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Your patient is a 32-year-old woman who reports that she is nine weeks pregnant. She is complaining of severe abdominal pain, slight vaginal bleeding, and shoulder pain. Abdominal examination reveals significant tenderness in the lower right quadrant. The patient is somewhat agitated and tachycardic. You should suspect developing shock resulting from which of the following conditions?
questions.explanation: Signs and symptoms of ectopic pregnancy include lower-abdominal pain that is often referred to the shoulder, abdominal tenderness, and rapidly developing shock. Although a ruptured appendix may be suspected, the first -problem to rule out (given the pregnancy history) is an ectopic pregnancy.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: What is the usual clinical presentation of placenta previa?
options: tearing pain and dark red bleeding
diffuse abdominal pain and slight bleeding
elevated blood pressure and diffuse pain
questions.explanation: Placenta previa usually presents as painless bright red bleeding that occurs in the third trimester of pregnancy. The blood may be contained within the uterus.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Which of the following findings or factors would make you opt for immediate transport of a pregnant woman rather than attempt delivery in the field?
questions.explanation: Because meconium staining in the amniotic fluid can indicate fetal distress, it may mean that the best thing to do is to transport the mother immediately. Choices a, b, and d suggest that birth is imminent and transport is not advisable. If you must deliver the infant when meconium staining is present, you should be prepared to provide immediate suctioning of the trachea and to intubate the child prior to stimulation, drying, warming, or positioning.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: During a normal delivery, you would suction the infant’s mouth and nose just after
options: the head is out of the vagina.
the head and chest are delivered.
questions.explanation: Suction the infant’s mouth and nose immediately after the head is delivered and you can access the mouth and nose. Remember to suction the mouth first, then the nose.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You are managing a patient with symptomatic bradycardia and multifocal PVCs. Suddenly, your patient slumps unconscious and goes into ventricular fibrillation. You confirm that your patient is pulseless. Your next action would be to
questions.explanation: Immediate defibrillation has been proven to be effective in terminating ventricular fibrillation. Even stopping for CPR in a witnessed event such as this may be more harmful than beneficial.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What is the correct method to stimulate respirations in a neonate?
options: Hold it by the feet while you slap the -buttocks.
Slap the soles of the feet and rub the back.
Let the cool air cause it to shiver a little.
Rub the head but avoid touching the fontanel.
questions.explanation: Stimulate a neonate by slapping the soles of the feet and rubbing the back. The infant should not be allowed to lose any body heat, and you should avoid touching the head to keep from putting any pressure on the fontanel.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: What is the greatest concern when a person experiences blunt force trauma to the head?
options: It can interrupt the integrity of the blood brain barrier.
It can cause a significant increase in intracranial pressure.
It can cause significant ringing in the ears.
It can lacerate the scalp, causing bleeding.
questions.explanation: Increased ICP after blunt force trauma to the head is potentially lethal and will need close monitoring and management both prehospital and within the emergency department.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Why must your assessment be especially diligent in an intoxicated patient?
options: Alcohol can decrease pain tolerance in the patient.
Alcohol can mask signs and symptoms of injury.
Alcohol can increase the patient’s willingness to cooperate.
Alcohol can decrease an injury’s effect on the patient’s level of consciousness.
questions.explanation: As a mild anesthetic, ethanol can reduce the patient’s perception of pain. It will be important to carefully and completely examine the patient for any unnoticed injuries.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: What are the blood vessels in the umbilical cord?
questions.explanation: The umbilical cord contains two arteries and one vein; only the umbilical vein is used for vascular access.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You respond to a 19-year-old female who is 25 weeks pregnant and is complaining of a severe headache and blurred vision. Upon initial assessment, you notice that she has substantial edema, especially in her feet and hands. She states that she has not been under the regular care of a physician during this pregnancy. What abnormality would you expect to see in this patient’s vital signs?
questions.explanation: This is toxemia, a hypertensive disorder of pregnancy.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You respond to a 19-year-old female who is 25 weeks pregnant and is complaining of a severe headache and blurred vision. Upon initial assessment, you notice that she has substantial edema, especially in her feet and hands. She states that she has not been under the regular care of a physician during this pregnancy. This patient is most likely suffering from the condition known as
questions.explanation: This patient is most likely suffering from preeclampsia.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You respond to a 19-year-old female who is 25 weeks pregnant and is complaining of a severe headache and blurred vision. Upon initial assessment, you notice that she has substantial edema, especially in her feet and hands. She states that she has not been under the regular care of a physician during this pregnancy. Treatment for this patient includes rapid transport without lights and sirens, an IV of normal saline or Ringer’s lactate, and oxygen. It may also call for the administration of which of the following medications?
questions.explanation: Magnesium sulfate is the appropriate medication for the suppression of seizures in a preeclampsia patient. Valium may help control seizures, but magnesium suppresses them.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You respond to a 19-year-old female who is 25 weeks pregnant and is complaining of a severe headache and blurred vision. Upon initial assessment, you notice that she has substantial edema, especially in her feet and hands. She states that she has not been under the regular care of a physician during this pregnancy. If left untreated, this patient’s condition may worsen and she may begin to experience which of the following?
questions.explanation: Seizures indicate that the patient’s condition has worsened. The condition is then called eclampsia.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Which of the following statements regarding febrile seizures is true?
options: Patients suffering from febrile seizures seldom need evaluation at the hospital.
Febrile seizures frequently occur in infants with fevers.
Febrile seizures signify serious brain disorders.
Febrile seizures occur because of a rapid rise in skin temperature.
questions.explanation: Febrile seizures occur in children because of a rapid rise in temperature and not necessarily the severity of the fever itself.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: You are caring for a 69-year-old AMI patient using online medical control. A bystander, who identifies herself as a physician, looks at the ECG, sees you beginning to administer epinephrine, and requests that you substitute atropine. What should you do?
options: Have the online physician consult directly with the on-scene physician.
Follow the directions of the on-scene physician who has assumed care.
Ignore the on-scene physician and follow the directions of the online physician.
Leave the scene and the patient in the care of the on-scene physician.
questions.explanation: Although the online medical control physician is ultimately responsible for the care the patient receives, it would be appropriate to allow the two physicians to communicate while you continue care of the patient per your online direction. You should never leave the patient unless the on-scene physician can be identified to your satisfaction and has agreed to assume care of the patient and medical direction has authorized you to discontinue care.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Bradycardia refers to a heart rate that is less than how many beats per minute?
questions.explanation: Bradycardia refers to a heart rate of less than 60 beats per minute.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Which of the following statements regarding falls among the elderly is most accurate?
options: The elderly have the highest incidence of falls.
Fall-related injuries represent the leading cause of accidental death among the elderly.
Falls account for the highest percentage of emergency department visits among the elderly.
A majority of falls are primarily related to infrapatellar bursitis.
questions.explanation: Falls kill more elderly Americans annually than any other trauma. It is important to carefully evaluate and manage these patients.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Scene size-up
options: is the responsibility of the driver of the EMS crew.
should be performed immediately after the initial patient assessment.
is necessary only when approaching a trauma scene.
questions.explanation: Scene size-up includes taking BSI precautions and scene safety. It should be done on all calls and is the responsibility of all responding providers.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Which of the following situations constitutes abandonment?
options: A paramedic yields control of a patient’s care to a physician who has just arrived on the scene.
A paramedic yields control of a patient’s care to an EMT while the patient still needs ALS-level care.
An EMT yields control of a patient’s care to a paramedic even though the patient only needs BLS-level care.
A patient refuses transport and the paramedic leaves after obtaining a signature on a refusal of treatment form.
questions.explanation: Abandonment means that a healthcare worker either terminates treatment inappropriately or turns care over to less qualified personnel.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: In which of the following situations is an EMS provider required to make a report to law enforcement?
questions.explanation: Most states require the reporting of abuse or neglect of children, spouses, or older adults; rape and sexual assault; gunshot and stab wounds; animal bites; and certain communicable diseases.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Which patient may be legally placed in protective custody by the police if he or she refuses treatment?
options: a competent 85-year-old man who has signed a DNR order at the nursing home
a patient who is drunk and disorderly and who refuses treatment for a head wound
an epileptic patient who has just recovered from a seizure but refuses transport
a diabetic who has just recovered from insulin shock and wants to go home
questions.explanation: Protective custody is used legally in cases of patients who are drunk, high on drugs, or a danger to themselves and others, when it is obvious that their condition is impairing their judgment.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: You are dispatched to the home of a dying patient who has signed a Do Not Resuscitate order. What should you do?
options: Contact medical control before providing any patient care.
Give only emotional support to the patient and family members.
Carry out all necessary care since such orders apply only to hospitals.
Leave immediately without providing any care for the patient.
questions.explanation: Contact medical control about the specific situation before providing care; this will allow you to provide the type of care that is palliative only. Some states have specific protocols to follow with DNR patients and may even provide various levels of care in a variety of circumstances.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Pharmacological management of postpartum hemorrhage may include which of the following?
questions.explanation: Oxytocin may help control postpartum hemorrhage.
questions.subject: Trauma

questions.text: Which of the following situations is most likely to be declared a major incident?
options: an accident involving a school bus and car with five patients
a fire at a chemical plant during working hours
a water accident on a state line with two people in the water
a fire involving an isolated single-family residence
questions.explanation: This incident has a hazardous-materials component along with the potential for lots of patients. All the other incidents may be severe but should not overwhelm the normal resources for the area.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: What is the major responsibility of the finance sector at a major incident?
options: to document the number of personnel and hours worked
to acquire resources needed to resolve the incident
to predict future needs resulting from the incident and provide for them
to define objectives for resolving the incident
questions.explanation: Finance is responsible for the financial accounting of the incident and may not be needed in a small-scale incident. In addition to tracking personnel, finance approves rental and purchase of any additional equipment needed for the incident. Logistics is responsible for acquiring resources, planning evaluates, and command sets goals and objectives.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: You are conducting triage using the START system at a major incident. You encounter a patient who is not breathing. After you position the airway, the patient begins to breathe at a rate of six respirations per minute. Into which category would you now triage this patient?
questions.explanation: When using the START triage system, you assess three parameters: respiration, pulse, and mental status (RPM). A patient with no respirations is considered dead or dying; if the rate is under 10 or over 30, the patient is immediate or critical. If the rate is between 10 and 30, additional assessment is needed (so you would then assess pulse, and possibly also mental status, before deciding on a category).
questions.subject: Airway and Breathing

questions.text: The set of duties and skills that a paramedic is permitted to perform during a medical situation is called
questions.explanation: Scope of practice is generally defined by state statute and defines what an EMS provider can perform.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: Drawing a patient’s blood without her permission may be an example of
questions.explanation: In most situations, unpermitted physical contact is a form of battery. Assault is the threat of bodily harm.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: For which of the following procedures is it -necessary to wear gloves, gown, mask, and -protective eyewear?
options: starting an IV in a moving ambulance
questions.explanation: Childbirth can be extremely messy and involve blood, amniotic fluid, urine, and feces, so maximum precautions should be taken to include gloves, gowns, mask, and eye protection. For routine IV starts and IM drug administration, gloves alone should be appropriate BSI. All of the precautions listed are recommended by USFA (United States Fire Administration) guidelines for bleeding control with spurting bleeding but are not necessary with minimal bleeding.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Which form of hepatitis poses the lowest risk to paramedics?
questions.explanation: Hepatitis A is enteric (or food-borne) and poses the least risk for healthcare providers. Hepatitis B virus (HBV) is the major occupational blood-borne pathogen risk for paramedics. Hepatitis C occurs most frequently in IV drug abusers and paramedics are at risk of contracting this disease from accidental needle sticks. Hepatitis D occurs only in individuals who currently have or had HBV infection, and who therefore also pose a high risk to EMS providers.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: The use of alcohol-based hand wash is encouraged in the field setting when hot water and soap are not readily available. To be most effective, you should also do which of the following?
options: Allow the skin to stay wet with the alcohol for at least ten minutes.
Remove gross matter from your hands, since alcohol cannot penetrate protein.
Place your wet hands quickly into gloves to allow the alcohol to penetrate fully.
Quickly dry the alcohol approximately 30 seconds after applying it.
questions.explanation: Alcohol can never take the place of thorough hand washing, but when hand washing is not possible, it can offer some protection. To be most effective, you should first remove debris, as -alcohol cannot penetrate large protein molecules. -Saturate your hands and use friction to remove particulate matter. Let your hands air dry immediately, but do not wipe them dry, as this diminishes the effectiveness of the alcohol.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: When you are caring for more than one trauma patient at a time, you should change your gloves how often?
questions.explanation: Changing gloves for each new patient contact prevents cross-contamination.
questions.subject: Cardiology

questions.text: You are interviewing Mrs. Rodriguez, age 87, who called EMS but seems reluctant to state her primary problem. Mrs. Rodriguez speaks English but is answering your questions slowly and haltingly. Her 20-year-old grandson is also present. In an attempt to obtain the necessary information, you should
options: direct your questions to the grandson only, since Mrs. Rodriguez is probably senile.
ask the grandson to translate for Mrs. Rodriguez since she does not understand you.
ask questions slowly, clearly, and respectfully, giving Mrs. Rodriguez time to respond.
stop the interview and ask the grandson to leave the room, then resume questioning.
questions.explanation: Treat Mrs. Rodriguez respectfully, and give her plenty of time to respond and plenty of encouragement, before you turn to her grandson for assistance or translation. If it appears that she is nervous about her grandson being present, you may then decide to ask him to leave the room, but unless his presence is upsetting to the patient, it is okay for him to remain.
questions.subject: Obstetrics and Pediatrics

questions.text: Which of the following is not typically a characteristic of an abused elderly person?
options: wealthy but refuses to give financial assistance to relatives
multiple physical and mental impairments, such as dementia
incontinent, mentally handicapped, and over the age of 65
physically handicapped, over the age of 75, and frail
questions.explanation: The typical abused elder is generally poor and dependent on the abuser.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of acknowledging and labeling the patient’s -feelings?
options: “Stop threatening me. I’ve never hurt you.”
“You seem angry. Do you want to tell me about it?”
“Anger is a hostile emotion. Let’s be more positive.”
questions.explanation: This is one way to acknowledge what a patient is feeling and to encourage him or her to express those feelings without passing judgment or getting too personal.
questions.subject: Medical

questions.text: Your ambulance is the first on the scene of a suspected radiation emergency. Dispatch has stated that a box containing radioactive material was found unattended and open in a park. No patients have been identified yet, and the police department is sealing off the area. When you approach the park, the three principles of safety to keep in mind are
questions.explanation: Limit your time of exposure, stay a good distance from the source, and place shielding between you and the source. Personal and crew safety is always your first concern.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Your ambulance is the first on the scene of a suspected radiation emergency. Dispatch has stated that a box containing radioactive material was found unattended and open in a park. No patients have been identified yet, and the police department is sealing off the area. If you are asked to set up a staging area for additional responding rescue crews, which of the following would be the best location?
options: upwind, close visual range, in an open area
downwind, close visual range, in an open area
upwind, no visual range, in a building
downwind, no visual range, in a building
questions.explanation: Upwind in a building helps to provide further shielding from the radioactive source. Since you will be able to communicate via radio, being able to see the scene of the emergency is not important in this situation.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Your ambulance is the first on the scene of a suspected radiation emergency. Dispatch has stated that a box containing radioactive material was found unattended and open in a park. No patients have been identified yet, and the police department is sealing off the area. In this situation, who has the responsibility for decontamination of patients?
questions.explanation: Decontamination should only be performed by specially trained hazardous-materials personnel.
questions.subject: Operations

questions.text: Your ambulance is the first on the scene of a suspected radiation emergency. Dispatch has stated that a box containing radioactive material was found unattended and open in a park. No patients have been identified yet, and the police department is sealing off the area. Which type of radiation is the most serious?
questions.explanation: Gamma rays are the most serious type of ionizing radiation.
questions.subject: Operations
